zh: 现在我们要谈谈那里可能存在的一些阻碍。

speaker: Unknown
en: One thing I'll note is, I should have mentioned this earlier, I just did this report on the job market.
zh: 我要注意的一件事是，我应该早点提到这个，我刚刚做了这份关于就业市场的报告。

speaker: Unknown
en: Interestingly, as of today at least, we have the most open PM roles globally at tech companies in three plus years.
zh: 有趣的是，至少截至今天，全球科技公司的 PM 职位空缺处于三年多来的最高水平。

speaker: Unknown
en: The last time it was this high was during COVID, basically.
zh: 上次这么高的时候基本上是在 COVID 期间。

speaker: Unknown
en: There are some good news there at least for the PM role.
zh: 至少对于 PM 角色来说，那里有一些好消息。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know.
zh: 我不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: Did that surprise you, Lenny?
zh: 这让你感到惊讶吗，Lenny？

speaker: Lenny
en: Yeah, it did, because there's always this sense that, why do we need PMs? What's the point? We have AI to just built stuff, and like-
zh: 是的，确实如此，因为总有这样一种感觉，为什么我们需要 PM？有什么意义？我们有 AI 就能直接构建东西，就像——

speaker: Unknown
en: It's the, why do we need PMs?
zh: 就是那种，为什么我们需要 PM？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's the point?
zh: 有什么意义？

speaker: Unknown
en: We have AI to just build stuff.
zh: 我们有 AI 直接构建东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And my sense, I've always been saying this, I feel like the PM skill is the most important, valuable skill of all the skills.
zh: 而我的感觉，我一直都在说这个，我觉得 PM 技能是所有技能中最重要的、最有价值的技能。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I know every role thinks this about their role.
zh: 我知道每个角色都认为自己的角色是这样。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, no, design.
zh: 就像是，不，设计。

speaker: Unknown
en: We design more than anything now.
zh: 我们现在做的最多的就是设计。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I feel like what you said where it's the deciding what to build, deciding if this is good and great and ready and prioritizing, I feel like that's what remains.
zh: 但我觉得就像你说的，那是决定要构建什么，决定这是否是好的、伟大的并且准备就绪的，并进行优先级排序，我觉得这就是剩下的部分。

speaker: Unknown
en: So to me, it makes sense.
zh: 所以对我来说，这很有道理。

speaker: Unknown
en: [inaudible 00:24:29] report came out in a fortuitous time because it was right before I was preparing to come on this show.
zh: [inaudible 00:24:29] 报告发布得恰逢其时，因为它就在我准备参加这个节目之前发布的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the three points I was going to make is builders are going to have the time in their lives.
zh: 我要提出的三点是，构建者将迎来他们人生中最美好的时光。

speaker: Unknown
en: Comps actually up and the Bay Area is essentially back in favor.
zh: Comps 实际上上涨了，湾区基本上重新回到了受宠的地位。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think those three things came very much loud and clear in your report.
zh: 我认为这三点在你的报告中非常响亮和清晰地传达了出来。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think the question around, hey, why are product managers doing so well?
zh: 我认为关于这个问题的疑问，嘿，为什么产品经理做得这么好？

speaker: Unknown
en: Why are there so many jobs?
zh: 为什么会有这么多工作机会？

speaker: Unknown
en: When in reality, a lot of product people that all of us know are struggling to find a role or we're hearing about these layoffs.
zh: 而在现实中，我们都知道很多产品人员正在努力寻找职位，或者我们听到了关于这些裁员的消息。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think it depends on how you define what a product manager is.
zh: 我认为这取决于你如何定义产品经理是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that for the first time, I think that we did have a dramatic shift in what's defined.
zh: 我认为第一次，我们在定义上确实发生了巨大的转变。

speaker: Unknown
en: We three years ago talked about, "Hey, product managers, there's lots of archetypes.
zh: 我们三年前谈论过，“嘿，产品经理，有很多原型。

speaker: Unknown
en: Some archetypes are actually more in favor.
zh: 有些原型实际上更受青睐。

speaker: Unknown
en: This was the IC conversation, the builder conversation."
zh: 那是 IC（独立贡献者）的对话，是构建者的对话。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Now what we're essentially saying is the information mover is essentially going to become a dinosaur.
zh: 现在我们本质上说的是，信息搬运工基本上将变成恐龙。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think about half of the product people that grew into the industry have that skill and superpower.
zh: 我想到大约有一半成长起来的产品人员拥有那种技能和超能力。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then there were a set of people who got into product because they like to build stuff.
zh: 然后有一群人进入产品领域是因为他们喜欢构建东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And folks like yourself who were a founder who that was sort of the motivation into joining the industry, and then they found themselves into this thing called product management.
zh: 像你自己这样的人，你曾是创始人，那是进入这个行业的动机，然后他们发现自己进入了这个叫做产品管理的领域。

speaker: Unknown
en: Those folks are builders.
zh: 这些人是构建者。 [[270] ] 这些人正是你的调查中被雇佣的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: And those people are the ones that are being hired by your survey.
zh: 那些人正是你的调查所雇佣的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think those product managers, everybody wants a builder.
zh: 我认为那些产品经理，每个人都想要一个构建者。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that interestingly, lots of engineers are builders, lots of designers are builders, lots of marketing folks are builders.
zh: 我认为有趣的是，很多工程师是构建者，很多设计师是构建者，很多营销人员是构建者。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think Builders Wanted is going to be the big tagline for the next couple of years.
zh: 我认为“招募构建者”将是未来几年的大口号。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it is so fun to build.
zh: 构建是如此有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'll bet you, and this is a prediction, that if you had to choose between preparing a podcast and sitting in Codex or sitting in Claud Code and working on your laptop, you would prefer the latter.
zh: 我敢打赌，这是一个预测，如果你必须在准备播客和坐在 Codex 中或坐在 Claud Code 中并在你的笔记本电脑上工作之间做选择，你会更喜欢后者。

speaker: Unknown
en: It is so fun.
zh: 那太有趣了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just like progress.
zh: 就像进步一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: You just make so much [inaudible 00:26:49].
zh: 你只是创造了如此多的 [inaudible 00:26:49]。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, look at this thing go.
zh: 就像，看着这东西运转。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's getting better.
zh: 它在变得更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just getting better.
zh: 它就是在变得更好。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, when I was a PM, I used to take a break and go change the light bulbs in the house.
zh: 我的意思是，当我还是 PM 时，我过去常常休息一下去换家里的灯泡。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the reason why I would change the light bulbs is that light bulb was broken, but then I replaced it and the light came on.
zh: 我之所以会换灯泡，是因为灯泡坏了，但我替换了它，灯亮了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And man, was that satisfying?
zh: 天哪，那太令人满足了吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Because in a product job, there is very few days of satisfaction because you don't really have the ability to see something broken that gets fixed.
zh: 因为在产品工作中，很少有满足感的日子，因为你没有能力看到某个坏掉的东西被修好。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just part of the challenge in having responsibility without authority until now.
zh: 这只是拥有责任却无权力的挑战的一部分，直到现在。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now you can participate in the joy.
zh: 现在你可以参与这种快乐。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can create a design.
zh: 你可以创建一个设计。

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't have to wait for the designer and convince the designer to go and actually work through this or to put your stuff on a backlog.
zh: 你不必等待设计师并说服设计师去实际解决这个问题，或者把你的东西放入待办事项清单中。

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't have to do that.
zh: 你不必那样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think that the builders are going to have the time of their lives, and I think they're going to be very focused on judgment.
zh: 所以我认为构建者将迎来他们最美好的时光，我认为他们将非常专注于判断力。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that they're going to invade other functions.
zh: 我认为他们将入侵其他职能。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think there's going to be...
zh: 我认为将会……

speaker: Unknown
en: When I started the group five years ago, we had, for the first two to three years, until we got to about 60, 70 people in this community of head of products, we had one founder.
zh: 当我五年前创立这个小组时，在前两三年里，直到我们在这个产品主管社区达到大约 60、70 人之前，我们有一位创始人。

speaker: Unknown
en: In the last 12 months, as we've gone to about 125, we have 14 founders.
zh: 在过去 12 个月里，随着我们发展到大约 125 人，我们有 14 位创始人。

speaker: Unknown
en: 14 people essentially have decided that their next job was not to take another product executive role, but to found.
zh: 14 个人本质上决定了他们的下一份工作不是担任另一个产品高管职位，而是去创立公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: Founding CEO is now open to us.
zh: 创始 CEO 现在向我们敞开了大门。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have one person, very senior person in my group who interviewed for a CHRO position because they wanted a product manager background for the CHRO position.
zh: 我小组里有一位非常资深的人，他面试了 CHRO（首席人力资源官）职位，因为他们希望 CHRO 职位具有产品经理背景。

speaker: Unknown
en: What is CHRO?
zh: 什么是 CHRO？

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the chief HR person in the company.
zh: 那是公司里的人力资源负责人。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you would never think that an HR person would be a former product manager, but now forward-leading companies are starting to say, "Hey, we need someone who can bring this obsolescence skill, this judgment skill, this empowerment skill, this builder skill to the function."
zh: 所以你永远不会认为一个人力资源人员会是一个前产品经理，但现在具有前瞻性的公司开始说，“嘿，我们需要一个能将这种淘汰技能、这种判断技能、这种授权技能、这种构建者技能带到这个职能中的人。”

speaker: Unknown
en: In fact, the function might be easier to learn than the other parts of the job.
zh: 事实上，这个职能可能比工作的其他部分更容易学习。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we're seeing that trend that product builders are going to have both a broader range of opportunities up and down the stack, and it won't be product managers, and engineers can become product leaders and product builders, and all of this blurring of the lines is going to take place.
zh: 我们正在看到这一趋势，即产品构建者将在堆栈的上下都有更广泛的机会范围，而且不一定是产品经理，工程师可以成为产品领导者和产品构建者，所有这些界限的模糊都将发生。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that unfortunately, the flip side has to be mentioned.
zh: 我认为不幸的是，必须提到反面。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that non-builders, and I think that anyone who sort of sees themselves as not loving, like sitting down and building something, and there may be reasons why, and we'll get into why you may not be in a position to find the time, let's say.
zh: 我认为非构建者，我认为任何那种认为自己并不热爱，比如说坐下来构建东西的人，也许是有原因的，我们会深入探讨为什么你可能没有时间，比如说。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you're not a builder, if you're like, "Look, I'm not really into tech."
zh: 但如果你不是构建者，如果你就像，“看，我并不是真的喜欢技术。”

speaker: Unknown
en: People say this all the time, "Oh, I'm not really into tech."
zh: 人们一直这么说，“噢，我并不是真的喜欢技术。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I just really found it to be a lucrative job.
zh: 我只是真的发现这是一份利润丰厚的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a role where my ability to communicate, my ability to move information, was where I ended up enjoying it.
zh: 在这个角色中，我沟通的能力，我传递信息的能力，是我最终享受它的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love team building, those kinds of nerds, which by the way, I love all those things.
zh: 我喜欢团队建设，那些书呆子，顺便说一句，我也热爱所有这些东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you don't love building stuff, you're in trouble.
zh: 但如果你不喜欢构建东西，你就麻烦了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I mentioned about half the people are in that camp.
zh: 我提到大约有一半的人属于这一类。

speaker: Unknown
en: So we're going to have that challenge where they're going to have to find, perhaps leave the tech industry.
zh: 所以我们将面临那个挑战，他们将不得不寻找，也许不得不离开科技行业。

speaker: Unknown
en: They might have to.
zh: 他们可能不得不这样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: It might be that they want to go build a new business outside of tech, but use some of these AI tools, or maybe they want to find a job completely foreign to tech.
zh: 可能是他们想去科技行业之外建立新业务，但使用一些人工智能工具，或者他们可能想找一份完全与科技无关的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think that this non-builder piece is sort of a mirror image of the builder growth story that we're seeing.
zh: 但我认为这个非构建者部分某种程度上是我们所看到的构建者增长故事的镜像。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's so many directions we can go here.
zh: 我们在这里有很多方向可以探讨。

speaker: Unknown
en: One thing I want to throw out, it's kind of a hot take, and this came from a chat I just had with Amol, a Head of Growth at Anthropic.
zh: 我想提出的一件事，这有点像是一个激进的观点，这来自我刚刚与 Amol 的一次聊天，他是 Anthropic 的增长负责人。

speaker: Unknown
en: So he's Head of Growth, he's a PM and he had this really interesting point that as engineers can do so much more, PMs are getting squeezed because they have to stay on top of so many things, so many features, so many ideas, so much talk, so many things being said at them.
zh: 所以他是增长负责人，他是产品经理，他有一个非常有趣的观点，随着工程师能做的事情越来越多，产品经理被挤压了，因为他们必须掌握这么多事情，这么多功能，这么多想法，这么多的讨论，有这么多事情冲着他们说。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's this push for PMs at companies to ship PRs, build stuff themselves.
zh: 公司里有一种推动力要求产品经理自己发布 PR，自己构建东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what we took away from that chat is the leverage that PMs often have is a lot higher, not spending time coding and shipping, but instead just staying on top of all this stuff.
zh: 我们从那次聊天中得到的收获是，产品经理通常拥有的杠杆作用要高得多，不是花时间写代码和发布，而是仅仅掌握所有这些东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And he's like, "We need more PMs now.
zh: 他就像，“我们现在需要更多的产品经理。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's so much for PMs to do because engineers are so fast."
zh: 产品经理有太多事情要做，因为工程师太快了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's still need for prototyping to explore ideas and ideate and get feedback and align.
zh: 所以仍然需要原型制作来探索想法、进行构思、获得反馈并进行对齐。

speaker: Unknown
en: But he had an interesting take that it's better I don't spend time shipping stuff.
zh: 但他有一个有趣的观点，即我最好不要花时间发布东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's better I do higher leverage work as a PM.
zh: 我最好作为产品经理做更高杠杆的工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: It depends what he means by shipping stuff because I think that if you have an engineering team of 50 people building things for your customers and you're like, "Hey, I want to be the 51st because that's how I get leveraged."
zh: 这取决于他所说的发布东西是什么意思，因为我认为如果你有一个由 50 人组成的工程团队为你的客户构建东西，而你就像，“嘿，我想成为第 51 个人，因为那样我就获得了杠杆。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "Eh, it's kind of a cheap knockoff of an engineer."
zh: 我就像，“嗯，这有点像是工程师的廉价仿制品。”

speaker: Unknown
en: If on the other hand, the thing that you're building is ways to stay on top of what the 50 people are doing when in the past that was building tickets and backlogs and all of the things that we used to do to figure out organizations, manage standups, that's the information overload that's happening.
zh: 另一方面，如果你构建的东西是掌握那 50 个人正在做的事情的方法，而在过去那是构建工单、待办事项清单以及我们过去用来搞清楚组织、管理站会的所有事情，这就是正在发生的信息过载。

speaker: Unknown
en: What we saw when the CPOs got together is all of the things they're building are ways to drive efficiency out of their product organizations.
zh: 当首席产品官们聚在一起时，我们看到的是，他们构建的所有东西都是为了从他们的产品组织中提取效率的方法。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're inside the building kind of development efforts.
zh: 他们是那种正在进行中的开发工作的内部。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm not sure that for the next five years, we'll continue to build software that way, but I think in the next two years, people are going to change the product operating system that they're working on.
zh: 而且我不确定在未来五年，我们会继续以那种方式构建软件，但我认为在未来两年，人们将会改变他们正在使用的产品操作系统。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we're already seeing companies that stand up and they say, "Hey, we've fully automated the way we do product reviews.
zh: 我们已经看到有些公司成立起来并说，“嘿，我们已经完全自动化了我们进行产品审查的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: We've fully automated the way we do product standups."
zh: 我们已经完全自动化了我们进行产品站会的方式。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So to his point, look, if there's 15, 10 times the amount of stuff happening, we need judgment to determine whether it's good.
zh: 所以说到他的观点，看，如果有 15 倍、10 倍的事情正在发生，我们需要判断来确定它是否好。

speaker: Unknown
en: These are good or bad changes.
zh: 这些是好的还是坏的变更。

speaker: Unknown
en: And right now, if it's manual, we're cooked.
zh: 而现在，如果是手动的，我们就完了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's a combination of hiring good product builders with judgment and then hiring increasingly folks whose entire job is to build the internal tools necessary to improve the decisioning, but it's not through hiring of humans and building management ethos, which is what it used to be in Zurp.
zh: 所以这是雇佣具有判断力的优秀产品构建者，然后雇佣越来越多的、其全部工作是构建提高决策所需的内部工具的人的组合，但这不是通过雇佣人类和建立管理理念，这在过去的 Zurp 是那样的。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's actually through technology, but it's a totally different way of building software.
zh: 这实际上是通过技术，但这是一种完全不同的构建软件的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's what gets people excited because they're like, "Wow, if I do this, I don't have to ever do a status report."
zh: 这就是让人们兴奋的原因，因为他们就像，“哇，如果我做这个，我就永远不需要做状态报告。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, that was a comment that was made.
zh: 我是说，那是发表的一项评论。

speaker: Unknown
en: I hate doing status reports.
zh: 我讨厌做状态报告。

speaker: Unknown
en: So now I just wrote something and my boss is happier because they get more detail.
zh: 所以现在我只是写了一些东西，我的老板更开心了，因为他们得到了更多的细节。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that to me is incredibly exciting, but it is a different direction of what you're trying to deliver than something that goes out the door.
zh: 对我来说，这非常令人兴奋，但它与你试图交付的东西的方向不同，那是送出门的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is such a good distinction and it makes all the sense.
zh: 这是一个非常好的区分，这完全说得通。

speaker: Unknown
en: Basically, it's make yourself scale through software as much as possible.
zh: 基本上，就是尽可能通过软件让自己规模化。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a big opportunity and it's fun.
zh: 有一个巨大的机会，这很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like you're building your own thing.
zh: 就像你在构建你自己的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's right.
zh: 没错。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's right.
zh: 没错。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think a few other things that I would just note around how things are going to change the next couple of years, I think that adults are still going to be needed.
zh: 我想关于未来几年情况会如何变化，我只想指出其他几点，我认为成年人仍然会是必需的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think companies are going to grow, they're going to grow quickly.
zh: 我认为公司会成长，它们会快速成长。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think those that are driving some of these initiatives are classic founders and AI is great at supplementing a lot of things, but I think judgment comes in the form of a combination of expertise and wisdom.
zh: 我认为那些推动这些举措的人是典型的创业者，AI 擅长辅助很多事情，但我认为判断力源自专业知识和智慧的结合。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think that increasingly I hear from my groups that, "Hey, I'm attractive to this company because I have wisdom, but I stay hands-on.
zh: 所以我认为我越来越多地从我的团队那里听到，“嘿，我对这家公司有吸引力，因为我有智慧，但我仍然亲力亲为。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have credibility with the founder so I can have a conversation with that individual in his or her language, but I have weight to my thinking.
zh: 我在创始人那里有信誉，所以我可以用他或她的语言与那个人交谈，但我的思想有分量。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have seen the movie.
zh: 我看过这出戏。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have experience that actually would help them as they take this turn."
zh: 我有在他们做出这个转折时能真正帮助他们的经验。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So adults are still going to be important.
zh: 所以成年人仍然会很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think pace will continue to go up, partly because people will want to feed the beast, the sort of LLMs that are running at night.
zh: 我认为步伐将继续加快，部分原因是人们想要喂养这头野兽，那些在夜间运行的 LLM。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think partly because there's just such an opportunity to keep moving so much faster.
zh: 我认为部分原因是有如此巨大的机会可以保持快得多的速度。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that's going to be dizzying and I'm not excited about what that causes for burnout.
zh: 我认为这将令人眩晕，我并不兴奋这对职业倦怠会有什么影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: Sadly, I think that geography and actually diversity is going to take a step back.
zh: 遗憾的是，我认为地理位置实际上还有多样性将会倒退。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that we were very good about driving more breath.
zh: 我认为我们在推动更多广度方面做得很好。

speaker: Unknown
en: As we grew the industry, we started looking at people who were from different backgrounds to populate it, including different locations.
zh: 随着我们发展这个行业，我们开始寻找不同背景的人来填充它，包括不同的地点。

speaker: Unknown
en: But I think that because the AI wave is so heavily coming from the Bay Area, and because companies are hiring fewer folks, they're hiring people that look and act like themselves.
zh: 但我认为因为 AI 浪潮如此大量地来自湾区，也因为公司雇佣的人更少了，他们雇佣的是看起来和举止像自己的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: So age, gender, I think, ethnic backgrounds, all of those are taking a hit.
zh: 所以年龄、性别，我认为种族背景，所有这些都在受到打击。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that we will have stepped back quite a number of years in the diversity of what we do.
zh: 我认为在我们所做的事情的多样性方面，我们将倒退好几年。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I worry, I mean, this is my personal worry, but I worry about that probably more now than I did in the last five years, because I think it's an underbelly of what we're seeing.
zh: 所以我担心，我是说，这是我个人的担忧，但我现在对此的担心可能比过去五年都多，因为我认为这是我们正在看到的阴暗面。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I don't think that anyone's intentionally doing anything, but I think when pace is so high, the fact is that women are having kids in those power years and they just don't have the time to allot to spending their nights and weekends in Claude Code.
zh: 而且我不认为任何人是有意做任何事的，但我认为当步伐如此之高时，事实是女性在那些能力旺盛的年纪生孩子，她们就是没有时间分配在 Claude Code 上度过夜晚和周末。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that's an impact.
zh: 所以这就是一种影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we don't talk about it as an industry, but it's absolutely true.
zh: 我们作为一个行业不谈论它，但这绝对是事实。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the last thing I would just say, and maybe this goes into some of the advice as well, is I think that one of the most surprising shifts we're seeing is your brands don't matter as much as how modern you are in your ability to deliver product.
zh: 然后我想说的最后一件事，也许这也涉及到一些建议，我认为我们看到的最令人惊讶的转变之一是，你的品牌并不如你在交付产品方面的现代化程度重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: In the past, I think for the last 10 years, it's like, have you seen the movie before?
zh: 在过去，我认为在过去 10 年里，就像，你以前看过这出戏吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: And even in this last podcast episode we did, Lenny, we talked about, hey, brands matter, you really need to make sure.
zh: 甚至在我们做的上一期播客节目中，Lenny，我们谈到，嘿，品牌很重要，你真的需要确定。

speaker: Unknown
en: Like personal brands?
zh: 像个人品牌？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, personal brands.
zh: 是的，个人品牌。

speaker: Unknown
en: "Hey, I've worked for this company, they've delivered products, they've been at scale."
zh: “嘿，我在这家公司工作过，他们交付过产品，他们有过大规模经验。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So then people are like, "Oh, that person certainly knows what [inaudible 00:37:54] doing."
zh: 所以人们就像，“噢，那个人当然知道 [inaudible 00:37:54] 在做什么。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's like, "Well, tell me about all the different experiences you've had."
zh: 就像，“那么，告诉我你经历过的所有不同体验。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But now if every way of building software and how we deliver product is completely alien to how it was in the last 10 years, how you delivered in that sort of version one is going to be less and less relevant.
zh: 但是如果现在构建软件的每种方式以及我们交付产品的方式都与过去 10 年完全不同，那么你在那个版本一中交付的方式将变得越来越不相关。

speaker: Unknown
en: So lots and lots of feedback that I'm hearing from interviews is, put you in a scenario.
zh: 所以我从面试中听到很多反馈是，把你放在一个场景中。

speaker: Unknown
en: What tools do you use?
zh: 你使用什么工具？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's your judgment?
zh: 你的判断是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: How do you think?
zh: 你怎么思考？

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not about five years ago, you shipped this thing, what was your thinking that went into it?
zh: 这不是关于五年前你交付过这个东西，你当时的思考是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so how modern you are now becomes the career advice, not did you pick up the established brands?
zh: 所以你现在的现代化程度成为了职业建议，而不是你是否选择了那些知名品牌。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because what if the established brands is very much working in a way that's not current?
zh: 因为如果那个知名品牌的工作方式非常不合时宜怎么办？

speaker: Unknown
en: You work there for six years, you come out, and it feels like you're in a totally different world.
zh: 你在那里工作了六年，你出来，感觉就像你处在一个完全不同的世界。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's why I think the next two years and these changes are so profound, and quite frankly confusing to people.
zh: 所以这就是为什么我认为未来两年以及这些变化是如此深刻，坦率地说让人们感到困惑。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is such an interesting insight that the logo, it used to be fancy to have all these logos on your resume, and now you're saying sometimes that may hurt you because that company's not seen as a very AI forward company.
zh: 这是一个非常有趣的洞察，关于那些 Logo，过去在你的简历上有这些 Logo 曾经是很风光的，而现在你有时会说这可能对你不利，因为那家公司不被视为一家非常 AI 前沿的公司。

speaker: Unknown
en: And second of all, people are looking for just like, "What have you actually done?
zh: 其次，人们只是在寻找，“你实际上做了什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Are you actually aware what's going on?"
zh: 你真的知道正在发生什么吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, I was just going to say, some of those biggest brands, it's hard to even talk about what you've done.
zh: 我刚想说，其中一些最大的品牌，甚至很难谈论你做了什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, if you're working at Meta and you spent two years and I managed to make this piece of this algorithm go a little faster, and by the way, it had a huge impact.
zh: 就像，如果你在 Meta 工作，你花了两年时间，我设法让这个算法的这一部分快了一点，顺便说一句，这产生了巨大的影响。

speaker: Unknown
en: And frankly, it was incredibly hard to navigate the hallways, and to make that decision and to find incrementality.
zh: 坦率地说，在走廊里周旋，做出那个决定并找到增量是非常困难的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, as someone who has worked there, I understand and respect that, and that is a promotable achievement, but it just falls very flat on a conversation where someone's living in the future and product is now totally different.
zh: 我是说，作为在那里工作过的人，我理解并尊重这一点，而且这是一个值得晋升的成就，但在某人生活在未来且产品现在完全不同的对话中，它就非常平淡了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that distinction is the thing that I want to just call out that, "Hey, that's happening every day right now."
zh: 所以这种区别是我想指出的，“嘿，这每天都在发生。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So what you're saying there, which is a really powerful point, is that the skills that used to be really valued in product managers are changing pretty substantially.
zh: 所以你在那里说的，这是一个非常有力的观点，即过去在产品经理身上真正受重视的技能正在发生相当大的变化。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And in fact, maybe almost looked upside down in terms of importance.
zh: 事实上，在重要性方面可能几乎是本末倒置的。

speaker: Unknown
en: In some ways, we start our careers as builders, many of us, and then we get taught that leverage, scale, organizing, streamline, empowering, enabling.
zh: 在某种程度上，我们中的许多人以构建者的身份开始职业生涯，然后我们被教导利用杠杆作用、规模化、组织、精简、赋能和启用。

speaker: Unknown
en: Don't do the build yourself, get others to do it, stop working on the car work on the factory.
zh: 不要自己去做构建，让别人去做，停止在那辆车上工作，去工厂工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is the maturation of our industry.
zh: 这就是我们要行业的成熟。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then you find out that, well, what if it turns out the scale can be done very differently and all we care about is your opinion and what you build.
zh: 然后你会发现，好吧，如果规模化可以以非常不同的方式完成，而我们只关心你的意见和你构建的东西，那会怎么样？

speaker: Unknown
en: That's very jarring and that's what we're living through right now.
zh: 这是非常令人震惊的，这就是我们目前正在经历的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: So let's follow this thread of specific advice to PMs right now, whether they're mid-career, senior, getting started.
zh: 所以让我们沿着这条针对 PM 的具体建议线索，无论他们是中期职业生涯、资深还是刚开始。

speaker: Unknown
en: What are some specific things that you think people should do to do well in this emerging future?
zh: 你认为人们应该做一些什么具体的事情才能在这个新兴的未来中做得好？

speaker: Unknown
en: Change is really hard for us.
zh: 改变对我们来说真的很难。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think as humans, we're not really designed to change very easily.
zh: 我认为作为人类，我们并没有被设计成能够很容易地改变。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you think about it, I was thinking about why is it hard for us to change when in some ways change can lead us to much better outcomes.
zh: 如果你仔细想想，我在思考为什么我们很难改变，因为在某种程度上改变可以让我们得到更好的结果。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're almost told to change less as we grow older.
zh: 我们几乎被被告知随着年龄增长要减少改变。

speaker: Unknown
en: When you're a kid, you fall down a ton.
zh: 当你是孩子的时候，你会摔倒很多次。

speaker: Unknown
en: But Lenny, when was the last time you fell down?
zh: 但是 Lenny，你上一次摔倒是什么时候？

speaker: Unknown
en: Not often, except my son loves to pretend like we're falling, so there's a lot of pretend fall.
zh: 不经常，除非我的儿子喜欢假装我们摔倒了，所以有很多假装的摔倒。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when you pretend fall, it's probably pretty jarring.
zh: 当你假装摔倒时，可能相当令人震惊。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's probably relatively new.
zh: 这可能相对来说是新鲜的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: For him, it's so fun.
zh: 对他来说，这太好玩了。

speaker: Unknown
en: He loves it, but yeah, for me, I'm like oh, my God, such a long ways down.
zh: 他很喜欢，但是是的，对我来说，我就像天哪，这么长的一段路。

speaker: Unknown
en: it's no fun, right?
zh: 这一点也不好玩，对吧？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that it's funny because you watch kids and it's normal for them to reinvent everything they do.
zh: 我认为这很有趣，因为你看着孩子们，对他们来说重新发明他们所做的一切是很正常的。

speaker: Unknown
en: "Oh, I'm learning to crawl.
zh: “哦，我在学习爬行。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, now this walking thing, crawling, that's not for me."
zh: 哦，现在这个走路的事情，爬行，那不适合我。”

speaker: Unknown
en: It's why kids learn languages so quickly because they are not afraid to make mistakes and they go through things much more rapidly.
zh: 这就是孩子们学习语言这么快的原因，因为他们不害怕犯错，并且他们更快地经历事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's why you teach your kids to learn how to ski early because boy, I've just made a decision.
zh: 这就是为什么你要尽早教你的孩子学会滑雪，因为天哪，我刚做了一个决定。

speaker: Unknown
en: Hey, I've never been a skier and I'm just too old to do it.
zh: 嘿，我从来都不是一个滑雪者，我现在太老了，不能做这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: "I think that we are trained to find a happy medium and then make as few changes as possible, find a partner, get settled down, find a job, try to stay.
zh: “我认为我们被训练去寻找一个中间立场，然后尽可能少地做改变，找一个伴侣，安定下来，找一份工作，试图留下来。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a failure to transition to a new job if you can avoid it.
zh: 如果你能避免的话，过渡到新工作就是一种失败。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's our entire model.
zh: 这就是我们的整个模式。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so we create also, in some ways, a mental block around this idea of reinventing oneself.
zh: 所以我们也创造了一种心理障碍，围绕着重新发明自己这个想法。

speaker: Unknown
en: When you are told to reinvent, to when you are told to change, your first thing you think about is it really necessary?
zh: 当你被告知要重新发明，当你被告知要改变时，你想到的第一件事是这真的有必要吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Boy, it seems exhausting.
zh: 天哪，这似乎让人筋疲力尽。

speaker: Unknown
en: I worked so hard to get here.
zh: 我努力工作才到了这里。

speaker: Unknown
en: Why would I need to?
zh: 为什么我需要这样做？

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think in the most inner psyche of some of us, its, that just wasn't the deal.
zh: 我认为在我们中的一些人的内心深处，这不是交易。

speaker: Unknown
en: The deal was I did what I was supposed to, went to school, I worked hard, I got a job, I built the brand, I got to be a manager, I'm making the income, I have the whatever it might be, the partner, the family, all this stuff.
zh: 交易是我做了我应该做的事，去上学，我努力工作，我找到了一份工作，我建立了品牌，我成为了经理，我有了收入，我有了无论它可能是什么，伴侣，家庭，所有这些东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: You're telling me after all this time, I got to start over.
zh: 你告诉我经过这么长时间，我必须从头开始。

speaker: Unknown
en: It sounds fun.
zh: 听起来很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: You read Twitter and you're like, "Wow, these people are doing all this crazy stuff."
zh: 你读 Twitter，你会说，“哇，这些人正在做所有这些疯狂的事情。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But in the back of your mind, it's like, "I just don't know why.
zh: 但在你的脑海里，就像，“我就是不知道为什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: Why did we get here?
zh: 我们为什么到了这里？

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't want to do it.
zh: 我不想这样做。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to go back to the world where I can just keep doing things."
zh: 我想回到我可以继续做事的世界。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think this block is actually at the heart of the matter today, and it's a skill reinvention, and it requires time, but time is so hard to find in your power years for the reasons we mentioned with so many...
zh: 所以我认为这个障碍实际上是今天问题的核心，它是一项技能重新发明，它需要时间，但在你的权力岁月里时间很难找到，因为我们提到的原因，有如此多的……

speaker: Unknown
en: Your goal when you're in your power years is to equally disappoint everyone in your life, which sounds like a horrible statement, but it's true.
zh: 当你在你的权力岁月时，你的目标是平等地让你生活中的每个人失望，这听起来像是一个可怕的声明，但它是真的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have six hours to give, eight hours to give, 12 hours to give, and you have 20 hours of demand.
zh: 你有六个小时可以给，八个小时可以给，十二个小时可以给，你有二十个小时的需求。

speaker: Unknown
en: What is your prioritization mechanism?
zh: 你的优先级机制是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: I am going to equally disappoint everyone, not disappoint one group.
zh: 我要让大家同样失望，而不是让其中一部分人失望。

speaker: Unknown
en: My parents will not be more disappointed than my kids, than my health, than my family, than my friends, than my partner, than my work, than my retirement account.
zh: 我的父母不会比我的孩子、我的健康、我的家人、我的朋友、我的伴侣、我的工作或我的退休账户更失望。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's essentially what your mindset is.
zh: 这基本上就是你们的心态。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's I need to equally disappoint everyone.
zh: 我需要让大家同样失望。

speaker: Unknown
en: Now you're telling me that the number one thing is to reinvent.
zh: 现在你们告诉我首要的事情是重塑自我。

speaker: Unknown
en: When I'm barely able to manage this disappointment algorithm.
zh: 而我几乎勉强能管理这个失望算法。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's quite dark that I think that that is the setup.
zh: 我觉得这就是设定的现实，这相当黑暗。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the worst part is these two other psychological factors.
zh: 最糟糕的部分是还有这两个其他心理因素。

speaker: Unknown
en: One is, okay, fine.
zh: 一个是，好吧，行。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'll take a week off and I'll go figure out what people are doing and what the latest is.
zh: 我会休假一周，去弄清楚人们在做什么，以及最新情况是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then it turns out three months later, that week off is now antiquated.
zh: 结果三个月后，那一周假期的见闻现在已经过时了。

speaker: Unknown
en: You got to keep doing it because the target keeps changing.
zh: 你得继续这样做，因为目标在不断变化。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not like, I went from not having a job to having a new job, and then I kind of grinned and bear and got through it, and now I'm back in flow.
zh: 这不像是我从失业到找到新工作，然后我咬牙坚持并挺过来了，现在我又回到了心流状态。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like, I'm never in flow because I got to keep doing this over and over and over again.
zh: 好像我从来都不在心流状态，因为我得一遍又一遍地做这个。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the most surprising observation I made is that the ones that were the best at working in the past, the ones that mastered the old game, find it the hardest to go through this reinvention stage.
zh: 我做过的最令人惊讶的观察是，那些过去最擅长工作的人，那些掌握了旧游戏规则的人，发现最难度过这个重塑阶段。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's this sort of shadow superpower thing that I talk about.
zh: 这就是我谈到的那种“阴影超能力”现象。

speaker: Unknown
en: The better you are at mastering one system, the less likely you are to sort of recognize the new one because your entire world is like, this is working to me.
zh: 你越擅长掌握一个系统，你就越不可能去识别新系统，因为你的整个世界就像是，“这对我来说奏效啊”。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so the weaker you are, the more excited you are about changing.
zh: 所以你越弱，你对改变就越兴奋。

speaker: Unknown
en: "Hey, what I'm doing isn't working, might as well change it."
zh: “嘿，我正在做的事情不起作用，不妨改变一下吧。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But if you're really good, you have no incentive to change, and perhaps even your employer sees you're doing great, and the company itself is in this moment.
zh: 但如果你真的很强，你没有任何动力去改变，也许甚至你的雇主觉得你做得很好，而且公司本身也正处于这一时刻。

speaker: Unknown
en: So all of these things, whether it's the shadow, whether it's the exhaustion, whether it's the time, whether it's the fact that the target's moving, creates this reluctance to reinvent.
zh: 所有这些事情，无论是阴影，无论是疲惫，无论是时间，还是因为目标在移动，都造成了这种不愿重塑自我的状态。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the number one piece of advice, to answer your question, is you have to have the courage, you have to believe, you have to have the power to essentially say, "Look, I know the way we work is changing, and I need to stay modern, I need to stay current.
zh: 但为了回答你的问题，首要的一条建议是，你必须有勇气，你必须相信，你必须有力量基本上说出：“听着，我知道我们的工作方式正在改变，我需要保持现代化，我需要保持与时俱进。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So I am going to cross that mental threshold, and I'm going to prioritize that above all else."
zh: 所以我要跨越那个心理门槛，我要将其置于一切之上。

speaker: Unknown
en: That crossing the threshold is the key.
zh: 跨越那个门槛是关键。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'll talk a little bit about how one does that, but I think that, I mean, if there was one thing to get away from today's discussion is every person listening to this podcast needs to find it in themselves to cross the threshold around embracing reinvention.
zh: 我会稍微谈谈如何做到这一点，但我认为，我是说，如果今天要从中带走一件事的话，就是每一位听这个播客的人都需要在内心找到力量，去跨越那个拥抱重塑的门槛。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is the world that we live in now.
zh: 这就是我们现在生活的世界。

speaker: Unknown
en: Nikhyl, this hits hard.
zh: Nikhyl，这番话真的很扎心。

speaker: Unknown
en: I told people you don't sugarcoat stuff and this is what we're experiencing now.
zh: 我告诉过大家你不会粉饰太平，而这正是我们现在正在经历的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: I am so excited to tell you about this season's supporting sponsor Vanta.
zh: 我非常激动地告诉大家本季度的赞助商 Vanta。

speaker: Unknown
en: Vanta helps over 15,000 companies like Cursor, Ramp, Duolingo, Snowflake, and Atlassian earn and prove trust with their customers.
zh: Vanta 帮助超过 15,000 家公司，如 Cursor、Ramp、Duolingo、Snowflake 和 Atlassian，赢得并证明其客户的信任。

speaker: Unknown
en: Teams are building and shipping products faster than ever thanks to AI, but as a result, the amount of risk being introduced into your product and your business is higher than it's ever been.
zh: 得益于 AI，团队构建和发布产品的速度比以往任何时候都快，但结果就是，引入到你的产品和业务中的风险量比以往任何时候都高。

speaker: Unknown
en: Every security leader that I talk to is feeling the increasing weight of protecting their organization, their business, and not to mention their customer data.
zh: 我交谈过的每一位安全领导者都感受到了保护其组织、其业务的压力，更不用说他们的客户数据了。

speaker: Unknown
en: ... their organization, their business, and not to mention their customer data.
zh: ……他们的组织、其业务，更不用说他们的客户数据了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because things are moving so fast, they are constantly reacting, having to guess at priorities, and having to make do with outdated solutions.
zh: 因为事情发展得太快，他们不断做出反应，不得不猜测优先级，不得不凑合使用过时的解决方案。

speaker: Unknown
en: Vanta automates compliance and risk management with over 35 security and privacy frameworks, including SOC 2, ISO 27001, and HIPAA.
zh: Vanta 通过超过 35 个安全和隐私框架自动化合规性和风险管理，包括 SOC 2、ISO 27001 和 HIPAA。

speaker: Unknown
en: This helps companies get compliant fast and stay compliant.
zh: 这有助于公司快速通过合规并保持合规。

speaker: Unknown
en: More than ever before, trust has the power to make or break your business.
zh: 比以往任何时候都更重要的是，信任有力量决定你的业务的成败。

speaker: Unknown
en: Learn more at vanta.com/lenny.
zh: 在 vanta.com/lenny 了解更多信息。

speaker: Unknown
en: And as a listener of this podcast, you get $1,000 off Vanta.
zh: 作为这个播客的听众，你可以获得 1,000 美元的 Vanta 减免。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's vanta.com/lenny.
zh: 网址是 vanta.com/lenny。

speaker: Unknown
en: What I think about as you talk about this is, I don't know if you saw this, they asked all the heads of the AI labs like Demis, and Sam Altman, and Dario, just like, "Okay, if everybody agreed to slow down and stop this stuff, would you do it?"
zh: 我在想，正如你谈到的那样，我不知道你是否看到了这个，他们问了所有 AI 实验室的负责人，比如 Demis、Sam Altman 和 Dario，就是问，“好吧，如果大家都同意慢下来并停止这些事情，你会这么做吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And they all said, "Yep."
zh: 他们都说，“会的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Which says a lot about just they're all freaked out a bit about how fast everything's going, how much is changing.
zh: 这说明了很多问题，表明他们都有点害怕事情发展得有多快，变化有多大。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then there's the reality of, but that is not going to happen because the game theory of it all is it doesn't work.
zh: 然后现实是，但这不会发生，因为这其中的博弈论是行不通的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You can't slow down because no one will actually slow down.
zh: 你无法慢下来，因为没有谁会真正慢下来。

speaker: Unknown
en: Whoever doesn't has a huge advantage.
zh: 谁不慢下来谁就有巨大的优势。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think there's just a big part of this insight is just like this is happening and there's nothing you can do to slow it down or stop it.
zh: 所以我认为这一洞察的很大一部分就像是，这正在发生，你无法做任何事情来使其慢下来或停止它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you could either try to pretend like it's no big deal and it's going to be all right.
zh: 你可以试着假装这没什么大不了的，一切都会好起来的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Or you could, as you said, get over this hump of just like, "Okay, I need to lean into this and see what I can do."
zh: 或者你可以，正如你所说，跨过这个坎，就像是，“好吧，我需要投入其中，看看我能做什么。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, and maybe there's a baby version of this that perhaps provides a bit more kind of solace and optimism.
zh: 嗯，也许这事儿有一个温和的版本，或许能提供更多的安慰和乐观。

speaker: Unknown
en: A baby version of this is that the way that people did, let's call it product management back in the day when HP, and Cisco, and AMD and these other companies built products that were physical hardware, they had long engineering cycles.
zh: 这个婴儿版本就是，人们过去做的方式，我们姑且称之为产品管理，在那个 HP、Cisco 和 AMD 以及其他公司构建物理硬件产品的时代，他们有很长的工程周期。

speaker: Unknown
en: There was a discipline called product management that was birthed, and that discipline had a very, very specific, very structured way of working that was essentially imploded when the internet companies came along.
zh: 那个时候产生了一种称为产品管理的纪律，而且那个纪律有非常、非常具体、非常结构化的工作方式，当互联网公司出现时，这种方式基本上就崩溃了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And Google, for one, built the APM program because they felt like, "Hey, this product thing that exists in the market isn't really what we need and we're not seeing success.
zh: 比如说 Google，构建了 APM 项目，因为他们觉得，“嘿，市场上存在的这个产品概念并不是我们真正需要的，而且我们没有看到成功。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And in fact, we're going to just train from the beginning this new breed."
zh: 事实上，我们打算从一开始就训练这一新品种。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that then birthed a lot of the current product managers, Microsoft and Meta and other big employers started to create a new version.
zh: 然后就诞生了很多当前的产品经理，Microsoft 和 Meta 以及其他大雇主开始创建一个新版本。

speaker: Unknown
en: And for the first few years, it was very jarring because everything that they did that they call product management resembled old product management pretty much in name only.
zh: 在头几年，这非常令人不协调，因为他们所做的每一件被称为产品管理的事情，除了名字之外，基本上都很像旧的产品管理。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there wasn't like you go to become a business school, you get a business school degree and all of a sudden you're a product manager, you would have to work.
zh: 并不是说你上个商学院，拿到一个商学院学位然后突然你就成了一个产品经理，你必须去工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you build some expertise, then you'd have to come in and help organize and collaborate and all this other good stuff.
zh: 如果你有了一些专业知识，然后你必须进来帮助组织和协作以及所有这些其他的好东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: The next couple years are going to be like that where every three months we're going to have more agents, different forms of judgment.
zh: 接下来的几年会是那样，每三个月我们将会有更多的智能体，不同形式的判断。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're blurring the lines on what the role and responsibility, people from different backgrounds coming in, people leaving the...
zh: 他们正在模糊角色和责任的界限，来自不同背景的人进来，人们离开……

speaker: Unknown
en: It's going to be chaos.
zh: 这将会是一团混乱。

speaker: Unknown
en: But as a couple of years go by, things will settle.
zh: 但随着几年过去，事情会尘埃落定。

speaker: Unknown
en: Companies will be built a certain way, and it won't be that you'll continue to change the way you're working.
zh: 公司将以某种方式构建，而且你不会继续改变你的工作方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: You'll have reached some form of optimization.
zh: 你将达到某种形式的优化。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're just not in that optimization in any way, shape, or form right now.
zh: 我们现在完全没有处于那种优化中，无论如何、以任何形状或形式都没有。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so the speed of iteration and how you think about, look, my job is not to move information.
zh: 所以迭代的速度和你如何看待，听着，我的工作不是移动信息。

speaker: Unknown
en: My job is to evaluate and I have to think through what does success look like, whether this makes sense for the system.
zh: 我的工作是评估，我必须思考成功是什么样子的，这对系统是否有意义。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a totally different skill.
zh: 这是一种完全不同的技能。

speaker: Unknown
en: But after a couple years, there's going to be some routineness to it.
zh: 但几年后，这会有一些惯例。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's going to be some training.
zh: 会有一些培训。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's going to be consistency.
zh: 会有一致性。

speaker: Unknown
en: The job you had next is going to look like the previous job.
zh: 你下一份工作看起来会像上一份工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: So all I'm making a point is that you need to cross the threshold to stay modern because things are changing, but I don't want to send the point that for the next 30 years, you're going to be on this merit round that's going to spin faster, and faster, and faster, and then you're just going to have to run off the merry-go-round and go vomit in the corner.
zh: 所以我想说的重点是，你需要跨过门槛以保持现代，因为事情在变化，但我不想传达这样一个观点，即在接下来的 30 年里，你将处于这个旋转得越来越快的优绩轮上，然后你只需要跑下旋转木马去角落呕吐。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's not at all what I'm suggesting.
zh: 这根本不是我建议的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm just saying that right now is the moment when if you love building, you have to stay current because you will be happier and you'll be more relevant.
zh: 我只是说，现在是这一刻，如果你热爱构建，你必须保持最新，因为你会更快乐，你会更有相关性。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you don't love building, you have to recognize the industry is moving away from you.
zh: 如果你不热爱构建，你必须认识到行业正在远离你。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is the key point.
zh: 这是关键点。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I don't think there's a person that's looking at the current way of building product.
zh: 而且我不认为有一个人在看待当前构建产品的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't think there's a person that's been on this podcast that would disagree with that statement.
zh: 我不认为有一个人上过这个播客会不同意那个陈述。

speaker: Unknown
en: That is a really empowering point to make there that this is not forever, that it's a couple years potentially, maybe, I don't know, could be a little bit longer, but it's not a forever thing where you have to do this.
zh: 这是一个非常令人受启发的观点，这不是永远，这可能是几年，也许，我不知道，可能会稍微长一点，但这不是一个你必须这样做的永远的事情。

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to lock in and sacrifice forever.
zh: 你必须锁定并永远牺牲。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is the moment to get on the rocket, to get on the ship.
zh: 这是登上火箭，登上船的时刻。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know, to not miss the boat, whatever metaphor you want to use.
zh: 我不知道，不要错过这艘船，无论你想用什么隐喻。

speaker: Unknown
en: Whatever transportation.
zh: 无论什么交通工具。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think that would make me feel better.
zh: 所以我想这会让我感觉好一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the other, I don't know, the good, again, just to remind people, it sucks to have to change.
zh: 我想另一个，我不知道，好的，再次提醒人们，不得不改变是很糟糕的。

speaker: Unknown
en: The good, as you shared earlier, comp has never been higher.
zh: 好的一面，正如你之前分享的，薪酬从未如此之高。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a lot of open roles.
zh: 有很多开放的职位。

speaker: Unknown
en: People that are embracing this are having a lot of fun.
zh: 拥抱这一点的人玩得很开心。

speaker: Unknown
en: Most PMs are sitting there all day waiting for stuff to happen, waiting for meetings and approvals and all this alignment and all these PR reviews.
zh: 大多数 PM 整天坐在那里等待事情发生，等待会议和批准以及所有这些对齐和所有这些 PR 审查。

speaker: Unknown
en: And now it's like you can ship stuff so often.
zh: 现在就像你可以经常发布东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's so much good that is happening.
zh: 有很多好的事情正在发生。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe just remind us again of anything else there just to inspire people to like, "Okay, this is worth it."
zh: 也许只是再次提醒我们那里的任何事情，只是为了激励人们，比如，“好的，这是值得的。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I think let's go to the individual you're describing.
zh: 我想让我们去找你描述的那个人。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's someone who's sitting, who's really afraid, who's a builder, who's like, "Man, my job is changing so rapidly and I'm so nervous and I have to think through what's my career going to look like."
zh: 有一个人坐在那里，真的很害怕，是一个构建者，他说，“天哪，我的工作变化太快了，我很紧张，我必须思考我的职业生涯会是什么样子。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But I tend to ask the question like, "Do you really love your day?"
zh: 但我倾向于问这样的问题，“你真的喜欢你的一天吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: If you put green, yellow, and red next to the meetings you had and did that for a week and then we looked at the color chart, I'll bet you the vast majority of PMs that are in a product organization, they show mostly yellow and red.
zh: 如果你在你参加的会议旁边放上绿色、黄色和红色，并这样做一个星期，然后我们看这个颜色图表，我敢打赌，绝大多数在产品组织中的 PM，他们显示的主要是黄色和红色。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm telling you, the ones that are moving into more of this build mode, it's mostly green and yellow.
zh: 而我告诉你，那些正在进入更多这种构建模式的人，主要是绿色和黄色。

speaker: Unknown
en: And yet, most of them are sitting very, very afraid feeling stuck.
zh: 然而，他们中的绝大多数人非常非常害怕地坐在那里，感觉被卡住了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So what I'm suggesting is how do you then transition mentally and then physically into a moment of going from this moment of fear and being on the sidelines to being in the game?
zh: 所以我建议的是，你要如何在精神上，然后在身体上，过渡到一个时刻，从这个恐惧和站在场边的时刻转变到进入比赛中？

speaker: Unknown
en: What I have noticed, and this is sort of a big kind of surprise to me, is there's a moment where they experience the first joy in using the new tools.
zh: 我注意到的，这对我来说也是某种程度的惊喜，是他们有一个时刻，在使用新工具时体验到了第一次快乐。

speaker: Unknown
en: Everyone has a story and it's always different, but it's super personal.
zh: 每个人都有一个故事，而且总是不同的，但这超级私人化。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's going to be like, "Oh, I was doing it a certain way, and then all of a sudden I built this thing."
zh: 它会是这样的，"噢，我以前是用某种方式做这件事，然后突然我构建了这个东西。"

speaker: Unknown
en: And oftentimes it's like, "My partner and I use this app that I built," or, "I built a chief of staff app to keep track of my inbox," or, "I now manage the lights in my house using this thing."
zh: 而且通常是，"我和我的伴侣使用我构建的这款应用，"或者，"我构建了一个幕僚长应用来跟踪我的收件箱，"或者，"我现在用这个东西管理我家的灯光。"

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's some silly thing.
zh: 而且这通常是一些傻傻的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And usually accompanies with a story like, "And I stayed up all night," or, "I spent a bunch of time talking to my friends or hacking away," or, "I just spent time talking to Claude about it."
zh: 而且通常伴随着一个故事，比如，"我熬了一整夜，"或者，"我花了一堆时间和朋友聊天或者在那钻研，"或者，"我只是花时间和 Claude 聊这件事。"

speaker: Unknown
en: Even my wife has a story about how she has this business that she's thinking through and she went from using the AIs to do the business plan, to actually do test market.
zh: 甚至我妻子也有一个故事，关于她如何思考她有这门生意，她从使用 AI 来做商业计划，到实际进行市场测试。

speaker: Unknown
en: And everyone has this moment of joy and then they're like hooked.
zh: 每个人都有这个快乐的时刻，然后他们就像上瘾了一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's like they caught a bug.
zh: 就像他们染上了一种瘾。

speaker: Unknown
en: And at that moment is when they cross the threshold between fear to joy and joy is the biggest anecdote to burnout and it creates opportunity.
zh: 而在那一刻，就是他们跨越从恐惧到快乐的门槛，而快乐是职业倦怠最大的解药，它创造了机会。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because the moment you have joy, the moment it doesn't feel like work.
zh: 因为你拥有快乐的时刻，就是它感觉不像工作的时刻。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think most product management feels like work if you're not building.
zh: 而我认为大多数产品管理如果不构建的话，感觉就像工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we are now moving into a world where product management will be building in joy, not work.
zh: 而我们正在进入这样一个世界，产品管理将在快乐中进行构建，而不是工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you just need to find a path to get there.
zh: 所以你只需要找到一条路到达那里。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the moment you do, the rest of your human psyche creates time, doesn't feel like you're disappointing others and builds energy because people have more energy than they realize.
zh: 而当你做到的那一刻，你的人类心理的其余部分会创造时间，不会觉得你在让别人失望，并建立能量，因为人们拥有的能量比他们意识到的要多。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're just so exhausted by the monotony of what was defined as product management.
zh: 他们只是被被定义为产品管理的单调乏味弄得筋疲力尽。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that is the number one piece of advice is, have you found joy?
zh: 所以第一条建议是，你找到快乐了吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Now there's a class of person who's like, "None of this is joyful.
zh: 现在有一类人会说，"这些都不快乐。

speaker: Unknown
en: I find the whole thing to be kind of boring, nauseating.
zh: 我觉得这整件事有点无聊，令人作呕。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't really like it."
zh: 我真的不喜欢它。"

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "Well, then you're probably not in for the next version of our industry."
zh: 我会说，"好吧，那你可能不适合进入我们行业的下一个版本。"

speaker: Unknown
en: And you should be respectful of that and you should be honest with yourself.
zh: 你应该对此表示尊重，你应该对自己诚实。

speaker: Unknown
en: But there's a lot of product people that are right now very anxious that actually are going to be happier once they cross the chasm and all they need to do is find a way to create that moment of joy, whether that's an app on their side, other app at your work.
zh: 但是有很多产品人员现在非常焦虑，一旦他们跨越了鸿沟，他们实际上会更快乐，而他们需要做的就是找到一种方法来创造那个快乐的时刻，无论那是他们自己这边的一个应用，还是你工作中的其他应用。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you're a leader listening to this, you should find those moments of joy in your staff because that's contagious and it gets people excited.
zh: 如果你是一个正在听这个的领导者，你应该在你的员工中找到那些快乐的时刻，因为那是会传染的，它会让人们兴奋起来。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's why I like doing what I do, I like building stuff and I caught the bug and I'm all there all the time.
zh: 这就是我喜欢做我所做的事的原因，我喜欢构建东西，我染上了那个瘾，而且我总是全情投入。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I try to find TV shows that I can vibe code in parallel to because I want to watch TV, but I want to be vibe coding at the same time [inaudible 00:58:07].
zh: 我试着找一些我可以一边看一边进行 vibe coding 的电视剧，因为我想看电视，但我想同时进行 vibe coding [听不清 00:58:07]。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's a good show?
zh: 什么是好的节目？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's a good show for vibe coding?
zh: 什么节目适合 vibe coding？

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, a lot of the Amazon Prime shows are good.
zh: 嗯，很多 Amazon Prime 的节目都不错。

speaker: Unknown
en: [inaudible 00:58:14].

speaker: Unknown
en: A knock on it.
zh: 吐槽一下。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're built on books.
zh: 它们是基于书籍改编的。

speaker: Unknown
en: These books are like, they're structured and all that.
zh: 这些书就像是，它们是有结构的等等。

speaker: Unknown
en: So Alex Cross or Jack Ryan.
zh: 所以 Alex Cross 或者 Jack Ryan。

speaker: Unknown
en: You know what's hilarious is... And when you get older, you remember shows that you love, but you don't remember the plot.
zh: 有趣的是... 当你变老时，你会记得你喜欢的节目，但你不记得情节。

speaker: Unknown
en: So one time I just binge watch a season of 24, the show that I loved back in the day.
zh: 所以有一次我看了一整季的 24，我以前很喜欢的节目。

speaker: Unknown
en: But frankly, I watched it and I kind of knew what was going on, but I vibe coded the whole time because it was like, "Hey, I'm paying attention here, but I'm staying engaged."
zh: 但坦率地说，我看的时候大概知道发生了什么，但我全程都在 vibe coding，因为就像是，"嘿，我在这里集中注意力，但我保持参与。"

speaker: Unknown
en: Wait, let's actually follow this thread.
zh: 等等，我们实际上顺着这个话题讲下去。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just like, what's your AI stack?
zh: 就像是，你的 AI 栈是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: What are you using to build, and what's something you built and something you vibe coded?
zh: 你在用什么来构建，你构建了什么，以及你什么东西是 vibe coding 出来的？

speaker: Unknown
en: I kind of am pretty all in on Claude these last three months.
zh: 我在过去这三个月里相当 all in on Claude。

speaker: Unknown
en: I was, for a month, pretty aggressive on Codex because I found some of their newer stuff to be, especially with the highest level of reasoning to be quite advanced.
zh: 我有一个月相当激进地使用 Codex，因为我发现他们的一些新东西，特别是最高等级的推理能力，相当先进。

speaker: Unknown
en: I find it hard to switch between tools I try to standardize to be direct.
zh: 我发现在工具之间切换很困难，我试着标准化以保持直接。

speaker: Unknown
en: Look, the things that I build, I build a bunch of web properties for my community.
zh: 瞧，我构建的东西，我为我的社区构建了许多网络资产。

speaker: Unknown
en: So anytime I see something that I can obsolete and code, I try to build code around it.
zh: 所以每当我看到一些我可以在代码中淘汰的东西，我就试着围绕它构建代码。

speaker: Unknown
en: So if you had 100 people, one natural thing you want people to do is to meet each other and 100 people can't meet 99 other [inaudible 00:59:42].
zh: 所以如果你有 100 个人，你想人们做的一件自然的事情就是互相见面，而 100 个人不能见其他 99 个 [听不清 00:59:42]。

speaker: Unknown
en: So you have to be thoughtful around who's the best person to meet and how do you match people up?
zh: 所以你必须仔细思考谁是最适合见面的人，以及你如何把人匹配起来？

speaker: Unknown
en: How do you make sure that you match people with people they haven't met before?
zh: 你如何确保你把人们和他们以前没见过的人匹配？

speaker: Unknown
en: What are their haves or their wants?
zh: 他们的拥有物或需求是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: That entire thing used to be me sitting down and thinking like, "Oh, Jay-Z would really appreciate meeting Annie, but I don't know if they've met before."
zh: 整个过程以前是我坐下来思考，比如，“噢，Jay-Z 会很乐意见见 Annie，但我不知道他们以前有没有见过。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Now I write software to do that.
zh: 现在我写软件来做这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: I write an agent that goes in and actually does matching.
zh: 我编写一个智能体进去，实际做匹配。

speaker: Unknown
en: I write an agent to figure out, "Hey, what are all the jobs that my head of products are hiring?
zh: 我编写一个智能体去搞清楚，“嘿，我的产品负责人正在招聘的所有职位是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: How do we make sure that we make those available, but then build a mailing list of folks who I think when they're interested in work can get matched up automatically."
zh: 我们如何确保我们能提供这些职位，但随后建立一个我觉得对工作感兴趣的人的邮件列表，以便他们能自动匹配。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So the next generation of recruiting.
zh: 这就是下一代招聘。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think a lot about using AI for content.
zh: 我经常思考用 AI 来做内容。

speaker: Unknown
en: So when I sit down, I have an AI that takes questions from people and then it's trained on my content and it gives answers.
zh: 所以当我坐下来时，我有一个 AI 从人们那里获取问题，然后它在我的内容上受过训练，并给出答案。

speaker: Unknown
en: But then I read those questions, evaluate the answers, and I'm like, "Hey, this is a theme I'm hearing."
zh: 然后我会阅读那些问题，评估答案，然后我想，“嘿，这是我听到的一个主题。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And then I sit down and I write down, when do the LLMs and I disagree, I go through.
zh: 然后我坐下来写下，什么时候 LLM 和我意见不一致，我就过一遍。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so all of these things or anything that I'm doing that I think I can replicate, I try to obsolete myself.
zh: 所以所有这些事情，或者我在做的任何我认为我可以复制的事情，我都试图让自己过时。

speaker: Unknown
en: When I started my first job, I asked the best engineer that I worked with, who still is one of the best engineers I've ever met, "What's the definition of a great engineer?"
zh: 当我开始我的第一份工作时，我问和我一起工作的最好的工程师，他仍然是我见过的最好的工程师之一，“一个伟大的工程师的定义是什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I thought it would be like, "Oh, someone who's got this degree or is well versed in this technology stack."
zh: 我以为会是像，“噢，一个有这个学位或者精通这个技术栈的人。”

speaker: Unknown
en: He's like, "Well, the best engineer I know is my dad."
zh: 他说，“嗯，我认识的最好的工程师是我爸爸。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And of course, this was before tech in that case.
zh: 当然，那是那种情况下的技术之前。

speaker: Unknown
en: And he's like, "And my dad's definition is still my favorite, which is an engineer is someone who obsoletes themselves from everything they do.
zh: 他说，“我爸爸的定义仍然是我最喜欢的，即工程师是一个让自己从所做的每件事中过时的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the definition of a great engineer."
zh: 这就是伟大工程师的定义。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I've taken that to every job that I've worked at.
zh: 我把这个带到了我工作的每个地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's funny because you'll run into people that are like, "Oh no, no, I don't want to obsolete myself.
zh: 这很有趣，因为你会遇到这样的人，“噢不，不，我不想让自己过时。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's my job."
zh: 那是我的工作。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I was like, "Ah, I think if obsolete there'll be a better job for me."
zh: 我会说，“啊，我觉得如果过时了，会有更好的工作等着我。”

speaker: Unknown
en: What AI has done that has basically put an [inaudible 01:01:57] on that.
zh: AI 所做的事情基本上是在这上面按了一个快进键。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you can obsolete by just asking the AIs to do this for you.
zh: 你可以通过让 AI 为你做这件事而过时。

speaker: Unknown
en: So why I'm saying that is my stack is what can I do to obsolete anything and everything I do on a daily basis?
zh: 所以我之所以这么说，是因为我的技术栈是，我能做什么来让我日常做的每一件事都过时？

speaker: Unknown
en: That is super cool.
zh: 那太酷了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love hearing these stories.
zh: 我喜欢听这些故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: What I find as the best tactic here is just solve your own problem.
zh: 我发现这里最好的策略就是解决你自己的问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Think about something that's not great in your day-to-day or something you just want to improve and just like... And it sounds so easy.
zh: 想想你日常中有些事情不太理想，或者你只是想改进的事情，然后就像……这听起来很容易。

speaker: Unknown
en: All you do is go to, I don't know, Lovable or install Claude Code, download Codex and just tell it.
zh: 你要做的就是，我不知道，去 Lovable 或者安装 Claude Code，下载 Codex 然后告诉它。

speaker: Unknown
en: I want to build a dashboard to control my Sonos and then it just tells you, walks you through everything you need to explore.
zh: 我想构建一个仪表板来控制我的 Sonos，然后它会告诉你，引导你完成你需要探索的一切。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so that's a pretty advanced thing to try, but it's just like English.
zh: 这是一件相当高阶的事情可以尝试，但这就像英语。

speaker: Unknown
en: It sounds like I have no know-how to do this.
zh: 听起来我好像没有这方面的诀窍。

speaker: Unknown
en: You just tell it.
zh: 你只要告诉它。

speaker: Unknown
en: Here's what I want to do, just like a person and it helps you figure it out.
zh: 这是我想要做的，就像对人一样，它会帮你搞清楚。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, and I agree with you.
zh: 嗯，我同意你的看法。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then I think the question is what skill do you have to have?
zh: 然后我想问题是，你需要具备什么技能？

speaker: Unknown
en: For a while I was like, "Oh, that's a systems engineering skill because you need to be able to train the AIs to go after things."
zh: 有一段时间我想，“噢，那是系统工程技能，因为你需要能够训练 AI 去做事情。”

speaker: Unknown
en: But I'm watching my wife and how she works, and she's not an engineer by any means, and she's getting tons of value.
zh: 但我在观察我的妻子以及她如何工作，她绝不是工程师，她获得了巨大的价值。

speaker: Unknown
en: You don't even need to be an engineer.
zh: 你甚至不需要成为一名工程师。

speaker: Unknown
en: You just have to be opinionated on what you want to see.
zh: 你只需要对你想看到的事情有主见。

speaker: Unknown
en: And know what you want.
zh: 并且知道你想要什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, know what good looks like.
zh: 是的，知道好的长什么样。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then the moment that you get it, it's when the light bulb turns on when it wasn't working before.
zh: 一旦你得到了它，就是当以前不工作时灯泡亮起的时刻。

speaker: Unknown
en: And now you sit down and there's this moment where it's like, "Hey, I used to have to go and work to do this, but now my agent's doing this.
zh: 现在你坐下来，有这样一个时刻，“嘿，我以前必须去工作才能做这件事，但现在我的智能体正在做这件事。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then what's the next agent I could do to make my life a little bit better?"
zh: 那么下一个能让我生活好一点的智能体是什么？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And that's where you catch the bug.
zh: 那就是你着迷的地方。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's such a powerful thought that your job can be much more joyful as a product manager, as any function, but we're focusing on PMs.
zh: 这是一个非常强大的想法，你的工作作为产品经理，作为任何职能，都可以快乐得多，但我们专注于 PM。

speaker: Unknown
en: Just like if you look at your pie chart today of how happy you are during the day, you can actually be much happier in this future.
zh: 就像如果你今天看你一天中快乐的饼图，在这个未来你实际上可以快乐得多。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that's hard for people to really think, just realize, "Oh, wow, okay.
zh: 我认为人们很难真正思考，只是意识到，“噢，哇，好吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: I can actually love this job so much more?
zh: 我实际上可以更热爱这份工作吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I did not imagine that."
zh: 我没有想象到会是那样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that it's because you're staring at change.
zh: 我认为这是因为你正盯着变化。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think change is such an alarm bell.
zh: 我认为变化就是一个警钟。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's hard to hear the voice that says the thing that you are changing or the things you don't love because change is so hard for us that it's so scary.
zh: 很难听到那个声音说出你正在改变的事情或者你不爱的事情，因为变化对我们来说太难了，太可怕了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I respect that.
zh: 我尊重这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think that if your listeners are able to sort of understand that, hey, there is a world which is better, but I have to go through the tunnel and the tunnel sucks.
zh: 所以我认为如果你的听众能够稍微理解这一点，嘿，有一个更好的世界，但我必须穿过隧道，而隧道很糟糕。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the tunnel may mean that I'm going to have to change jobs.
zh: 而且隧道可能意味着我将不得不换工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: My prediction is the vast majority of people that are listening to this will be in a different job in the next five years because they will either choose to move to something because that company will struggle to stay modern just like we're describing.
zh: 我的预测是，绝大多数听这个节目的人在未来五年内会从事不同的工作，因为他们要么选择转向某样东西，因为那家公司将像我们描述的那样难以保持现代化。

speaker: Unknown
en: People need to stay modern, companies need to stay modern, or their companies are going to be shedding and returning off staff.
zh: 人们需要保持现代化，公司需要保持现代化，否则他们的公司将要裁员并削减人员。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so because change is apparent, I think it's just so deafening that people are very much gun shy to be excited about something that's forced on them.
zh: 所以因为变化是显而易见的，我认为它是如此震耳欲聋，以至于人们对被迫兴奋的事物非常畏缩。

speaker: Unknown
en: And let's be honest, it's been forced on everyone and it's not something that they chose.
zh: 老实说，这是强加给每个人的，不是他们自己选择的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's why I have a lot of empathy.
zh: 所以这就是为什么我有很大的同理心。

speaker: Unknown
en: But when I talk to people, I try to dig through, forget what's happened and why.
zh: 但当我与人交谈时，我试图深挖，忘记发生了什么以及为什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: How do we make the best out of it?
zh: 我们如何从中获得最大的好处？

speaker: Unknown
en: And the best is pretty good.
zh: 最好的结果相当好。

speaker: Unknown
en: So say we've convinced a listener, "Okay, I need to make a change.
zh: 所以假设我们说服了一位听众，“好吧，我需要做出改变。

speaker: Unknown
en: I need to lean into this.
zh: 我需要拥抱这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I need to take this seriously.
zh: 我需要认真对待这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I can't just sit back and hope it all works out okay."
zh: 我不能只是坐视不理，希望一切都会好起来。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And then they maybe found a moment of joy.
zh: 然后他们也许找到了片刻的乐趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: They built something that's super cool and just like, "Hey, check this out."
zh: 他们构建了一个非常酷的东西，就像这样，“嘿，看看这个。”

speaker: Unknown
en: What other advice do you have for folks listening to help them be to survive...
zh: 对于正在听节目的听众，你还有什么其他建议能帮助他们……

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know, let's not say survive, but just thrive in this future that is emerging for product people.
zh: 我不知道，我们别说生存，而是在这个正在为产品人员涌现的未来中茁壮成长。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, I like thrive.
zh: 是的，我喜欢茁壮成长这个词。

speaker: Unknown
en: I definitely think that you find that moment of joy, you have the engineering mindset, which is, "Hey, I want to obsolete myself on something that I do today."
zh: 我确实认为当你找到了那个快乐的时刻，你拥有工程师思维，那就是，“嘿，我想让我今天做的某件事过时。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Again, reduce the less joyful parts of my job is a good starting point.
zh: 同样，减少我工作中不那么快乐的部分是一个很好的起点。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that you have to find ability to increase pace.
zh: 我认为你必须找到提高节奏的能力。

speaker: Unknown
en: The next two years requires a lot of fire in the belly, a lot of agency that I know everyone talks about.
zh: 未来两年需要很多激情，很多每个人都在谈论的智能体。

speaker: Unknown
en: So for example, if you were to leave a job and start a new job, you probably don't view the new job year one at the same pace that you viewed year five in your last job.
zh: 所以举个例子，如果你辞掉一份工作并开始一份新工作，你可能不会以看待上一份工作第五年的节奏来看待新工作的第一年。

speaker: Unknown
en: You need to kind of bring it.
zh: 你需要有点全力以赴。

speaker: Unknown
en: If you have a new relationship and if you've come off of a long-term relationship, my suspicion is your first year of that relationship, you put your best foot on.
zh: 如果你有一段新的关系，而且如果你刚结束一段长期关系，我怀疑那段关系的第一年，你会展现最好的自己。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is what I'm asking.
zh: 这就是我要求的。

speaker: Unknown
en: You got to find that reserve.
zh: 你必须找到那种储备。

speaker: Unknown
en: You've got to make time.
zh: 你必须腾出时间。

speaker: Unknown
en: You've got to maybe disappoint others in a way that you haven't in the past in order to create time for you to stay current, find that joy, obsolete yourself from the things that are not worth it.
zh: 你可能必须以你过去没有的方式让他人失望，以便为自己腾出时间保持与时俱进，找到那份快乐，从那些不值得的事情中让自己过时。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the other thing you have to do is you have to swallow your ego.
zh: 我认为你必须做的另一件事是放下你的自我。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't want a single person saying, "Hey, I was a X, Y, Z leader.
zh: 我不想听到任何一个人说，“嘿，我是 X、Y、Z 领导者。

speaker: Unknown
en: I would only consider roles at that same level."
zh: 我只会考虑那个级别的职位。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I want people, this is an extension of our last conversation, is not only is it in vogue to be hands-on and I see it's kind of a necessity, I need everyone to sort of say, "Look, if everything's changing, it doesn't matter what we've done the past.
zh: 我希望人们，这是我们上一次对话的延伸，不仅动手实践是流行的，而且我认为这是一种必需，我需要每个人稍微说，“看，如果一切都在变，我们过去做过的事情并不重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: That brand doesn't matter as we discussed."
zh: 正如我们所讨论的，那个品牌并不重要。”

speaker: Unknown
en: You have to have an egoless perspective of how to stay current.
zh: 你必须对如何保持与时俱进有一个无自我的视角。

speaker: Unknown
en: And you would be not only willing, but actually look for ways to even take something smaller in order to make sure that you're kind of going through the tunnel correctly.
zh: 你不仅要愿意，而且实际上要寻找甚至接受更小的事情的方法，以确保你正确地穿过隧道。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then ultimately, part of the way we swallow that ego is you stay long-term focused.
zh: 最终，我们要放下自我的一部分方式是保持长期关注。

speaker: Unknown
en: You say, "Look, if the way we build product is changing so aggressively, my job is to spend the next couple of years being on the boat that's leaving the station and going to the new world.
zh: 你会说，“看，如果我们构建产品的方式变化如此剧烈，我的工作就是在接下来的几年里登上离开车站前往新世界的船。

speaker: Unknown
en: Once I get to the new world, the cream rises to the top, I'll take my skills, my leadership, and I'll go through it.
zh: 一旦我到达新世界，佼佼者会浮出水面，我会带着我的技能，我的领导力，我会通过它。

speaker: Unknown
en: But in the next few years, if everything's changing, I definitely want to be current."
zh: 但在未来几年，如果一切都在变，我绝对想要保持与时俱进。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And that means you have to have a long-term focus.
zh: 这意味着你必须有一个长期的关注点。

speaker: Unknown
en: My property is called the skip, of the community and everything.
zh: 我的财产被称为社区和其他一切的跳过。

speaker: Unknown
en: They all kind of come down to this word called the skip.
zh: 它们都归结为这个词，叫做跳过。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I chose that word because the best career advice is always not thinking about the next move, but the move after.
zh: 我选择那个词是因为最好的职业建议总是不考虑下一步，而是考虑那之后的一步。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's the skip job?
zh: 什么是跳过的工作？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's the skip opportunity?
zh: 什么是跳过的机会？

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, in this world, it's not about what's an opportunity now and what's the journey.
zh: 嗯，在这个世界里，这不在于现在的机会是什么，也不在于旅程是什么。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's about making sure that your skip opportunity is saved and you are able to get that high salary, you're able to get one of those premier builder jobs, and that's the world that you have to have and the mindset you have to have.
zh: 而在于确保你的跳过机会被保留下来，并且你能够获得那份高薪，你能够获得那些首要的构建者工作之一，那就是你必须拥有的世界和你必须拥有的心态。

speaker: Unknown
en: Something you mentioned earlier was really interesting that in the next five years, you predict that most people's jobs will be very different.
zh: 你之前提到的一些东西真的很有趣，即在未来五年内，你预测大多数人的工作将会非常不同。

speaker: Unknown
en: What are you imagining there?
zh: 你在那里想象的是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Will there still be this product manager role?
zh: 还会有这个产品经理角色吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Will PMs need to move closer to engineering?
zh: PM 们需要更接近工程领域吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: What's the Venn diagrams or spectrum you're seeing of where current PMs may go?
zh: 你看到的关于当前 PM 可能去向的韦恩图或光谱是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, I definitely think that there's a world where PMs go to every industry as what I would call the agents of change because the PMs are the ones that can talk.
zh: 是的，我绝对认为有一个世界，PM 们会像我所说的变革的智能体一样进入每个行业，因为 PM 是那些能交谈的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: They're the ones that have the broadest view of the organization, but they look at it through the technical lens.
zh: 他们是那些对组织有最广泛看法的人，但他们是透过技术镜头来看待它的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And my hope is that most of our PMs are the first to the tools, and hence they're the change agents within their company.
zh: 我的希望是我们的大多数 PM 都是第一个接触工具的人，因此他们是公司内部的变革推动者。

speaker: Unknown
en: So let's just play this out.
zh: 那么我们就来推演一下。

speaker: Unknown
en: In the next year, we start to see all these product organizations changing the way they build product.
zh: 在明年，我们开始看到所有这些产品组织改变他们构建产品的方式。

speaker: Unknown
en: And all of a sudden, the way we build product is gentrified and really, really thoughtful and really far along.
zh: 突然之间，我们构建产品的方式变得高档化了，真的、真的很深思熟虑，并且真的很超前。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then 12 months later, the marketing, the sales organization, the HVAC company that was bought by the private equity firm, the school down the road, everything's like, "Boy, we're not current.
zh: 然后在 12 个月后，市场、销售组织、被私募股权公司收购的暖通空调公司、路边的学校，一切都像是，“哎呀，我们跟不上时代了。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're going to get obsolete.
zh: 我们要过时了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Who do we bring in to be in charge of this change?
zh: 我们应该请谁来负责这个变化？

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, this person works in the future.
zh: 嗯，这个人工作在未来。

speaker: Unknown
en: We need to get to future."
zh: 我们需要进入未来。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So I'm really bullish that product leaders are going to be like those dandelion, whatever you call it, seeds when you blow it and they just go everywhere.
zh: 所以我非常看好产品领导者会像那些蒲公英，不管你叫它什么，种子，当你吹它时它们就到处飞。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have that vision.
zh: 我有那个愿景。

speaker: Unknown
en: Meanwhile, I think a lot of people going into product might be coming from design, might be coming from data science, might be coming from engineering.
zh: 同时，我认为许多进入产品领域的人可能来自设计，可能来自数据科学，可能来自工程。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because the ones that have judgment, the ones that can talk, the ones that want to stay current, they might be like, "Well, I don't necessarily want to just stay in my lane.
zh: 因为那些有判断力、那些能交谈、那些想要保持最新的人，他们可能会像，“嗯，我不一定只想待在我的车道里。

speaker: Unknown
en: I have a point of view as to how this should work."
zh: 我对这应该如何运作有一个观点。”

speaker: Unknown
en: As you said, from a designer point of view, designers may have a lot of those skills and opinions on what it should look like.
zh: 正如你所说，从设计师的角度来看，设计师可能有很多关于它应该是什么样子的技能和意见。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe their platform won't be the pixels or the visuals, they will be the product itself and they can adjacently move into product.
zh: 也许他们的平台不会是像素或视觉效果，他们将是产品本身，他们可以相邻地进入产品领域。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think you're going to see this crazy influx and then this amazing kind of exodus.
zh: 所以我认为你会看到这种疯狂的涌入，然后是这种惊人的某种外流。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then you'll also see folks that are like, "Ooh, I don't know if I can get in."
zh: 然后你也会看到那些像，“哦，我不知道我能不能进去”的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I know that's a confusing kind of set of three changes, but that explains why so many people have anxieties.
zh: 所以我知道这是一套令人困惑的三种变化，但这解释了为什么那么多人有焦虑。

speaker: Unknown
en: All three of these are happening instantaneously.
zh: 这三种变化都在瞬间发生。

speaker: Unknown
en: There's a line.
zh: 有一个条目。

speaker: Unknown
en: They thought they buried us.
zh: 他们以为他们埋葬了我们。

speaker: Unknown
en: They didn't know that we were seeds that I think about.
zh: 他们不知道我们是我想象中的种子。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love that.
zh: 我喜欢那个。

speaker: Unknown
en: We're into a lot of the darker...
zh: 我们进入了更多更黑暗的...

speaker: Lenny
en: Speaking of that, there's this hilarious tweet that just came out the other day that I have here-
zh: 说到这点，前几天刚出来一条有趣的推文，我这里有的——

speaker: Unknown
en: There's this hilarious tweet that just came out the other day that I have here, where this guy describes the only four jobs that will remain in the future.
zh: 前几天刚出来一条有趣的推文，我这里有的，这家伙描述了未来仅存的四个工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the only four jobs that exist in the future: one is product engineer vibe-code or PM slop-cannon.
zh: 所以未来仅存的四个工作：一个是产品工程师 vibe-coding 或 PM slop-cannon。

speaker: Unknown
en: Number two is security SRE infra person.
zh: 第二个是安全 SRE 基础设施人员。

speaker: Unknown
en: Number three is hot people, which is getting people to buy your stuff, customer service.
zh: 第三个是热门人物，也就是让人们买你的东西，客户服务。

speaker: Unknown
en: Remember, there are many ways to be... oh no, here's how they put it: "Present a easy UX to the world" is that category.
zh: 记住，有很多方法可以成为……噢不，他们是这么说的：“向世界展示简单的 UX”就是那个类别。

speaker: Unknown
en: And then grownups, which is your point of just like adults.
zh: 然后是成年人，也就是你刚才说的像成年人那样的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think there's some truth to this.
zh: 所以我认为这其中有一些道理。

speaker: Unknown
en: I read that and I agree with that.
zh: 我读了那个并且我同意。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, I think the adults exist and I think anyone that's the top of their field that can talk and that essentially is opinionated and can go broad, that's kind of the third point of hot people.
zh: 我的意思是，我认为成年人存在，我认为任何在其领域顶尖、能交谈、并且本质上是有主见的并且可以涉猎广泛的人，那都是第三类热门人物。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's actually... that reminds me.
zh: 所以这实际上是……这让我想起了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So Amol, the head of growth at Anthropic, made this really interesting point that so much of his time now, more and more, is alignment as a product manager.
zh: 所以 Anthropic 的增长负责人 Amol 提出了这个非常有趣的观点，即他现在越来越多的时间是作为产品经理的一致性。

speaker: Unknown
en: And we were joking, what's the harder alignment problem, aligning people in a company or AI's AGI?
zh: 我们在开玩笑，更难的对齐问题是什么，是对齐公司里的人还是 AI 的 AGI？

speaker: Unknown
en: And it feels like that's a remaining... and it's part of what you described, somebody that can get shit done, a change agent.
zh: 感觉这是一个遗留的……这也是你描述的一部分，也就是那个能把事搞定的人，一个变革推动者。

speaker: Unknown
en: And a big part of that is what a PM often is doing a lot of their time, is creating alignment internally around what we will be doing, what we're prioritizing, that kind of thing.
zh: 其中很大一部分是 PM 经常花很多时间在做的事情，就是在内部围绕我们要做什么、我们要优先考虑什么这类事情建立对齐。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that if you dissect what alignment looks like, a lot of alignment was getting people the right information, the ground-level truth.
zh: 我认为如果你剖析对齐是什么样的，很多对齐工作是为人们提供正确的信息，基本的事实。

speaker: Unknown
en: That problem is dramatically better.
zh: 那个问题现在好多了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that part of the job, frankly, just absolutely sucked.
zh: 坦率地说，那部分工作真是糟透了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Whether it was the status report or...
zh: 无论是状态报告还是……

speaker: Unknown
en: I was on projects where there was docs that were sent to me, and then I would change the doc to then send it to my boss, and then that boss would send it to... and it was just this movement of information where the ground truth was buried in some part of the organization, and then there were all these people that were putting on their spin.
zh: 我参与过一些项目，有人发文档给我，然后我会修改文档再发给我的老板，然后那个老板会把它发给……这只是信息的流转，基本的事实被埋在组织的某个部分，然后有这么多人在那里添油加醋。

speaker: Unknown
en: That, I think, is changing.
zh: 我认为那正在改变。

speaker: Unknown
en: But to your point, now someone has to decide, and someone has to have an opinion on what to fight for.
zh: 但正如你所说，现在必须有人做决定，必须有人对要争取什么有看法。

speaker: Unknown
en: And that conversation is now much clearer to have.
zh: 那样的对话现在清晰多了。

speaker: Unknown
en: Because you know the ground truth, there's less spin.
zh: 因为你知道基本的事实，少了一些添油加醋。

speaker: Unknown
en: The CEO can literally ask their agent, "Well, what is the situation?
zh: CEO 可以直接问他们的智能体，“好吧，情况是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: How is this performing?
zh: 这个表现如何？

speaker: Unknown
en: What does this customer really want?
zh: 这个客户到底想要什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: How does this affect the system?
zh: 这会如何影响系统？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is this really something that we want to change the product to enable?"
zh: 这真的是我们想要改变产品去启用的东西吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: So that conversation can happen, but now people can fight it out with real credence.
zh: 所以那种对话可以发生，但现在人们可以带着真正的可信度去争论。

speaker: Unknown
en: And if you're a product person that really does have an opinion and wants to make a point, you have a forum.
zh: 如果你是一个确实有观点并想阐述论点的产品人，你有一个论坛。

speaker: Unknown
en: So alignment won't go away.
zh: 所以对齐不会消失。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's not going to have as much theatrics.
zh: 它不会有那么多戏剧化的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think a lot of companies that are larger do have theatrics that I think AIs are going to remove.
zh: 我认为许多大公司确实有一些戏剧化的东西，我认为 AI 将会消除这些东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I don't know of many PMs, there's some that lived for the theatrics, but frankly, most of them were like, "This just seems like a fricking waste of time."
zh: 我不知道很多 PM，有一些人以此为生，但坦率地说，他们大多数人都说，“这看起来简直是浪费时间。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, it's so telling that 80%, I bet you, this is a statement, 80%, if you truly ask your PM, "Do you really want that boss's boss job, or do you want their day?
zh: 我的意思是，这太能说明问题了，80%，我敢打赌，这是一个陈述，80%，如果你真正问你的 PM，“你真的想要那个老板的老板的工作吗，还是你想要他们的日子？

speaker: Unknown
en: Not the pay, not the credibility, not the stature, but do you want their back-to-back meetings?"
zh: 不是薪水，不是信誉，不是地位，而是你想要他们背靠背的会议吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: I bet you most of them will say no.
zh: 我敢打赌他们大多数人会说不。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what I'm suggesting to you is that answer is going to change in the next two years.
zh: 我给你的建议是，这个答案在未来两年内会改变。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's really also interesting is, as much as this role is changing, engineering is changing even more.
zh: 真正有趣的还有，尽管这个角色在改变，工程改变得更多。

speaker: Unknown
en: And what I feel like is that the engineers that will continue to thrive are basically going to become more PM-y, because the coding part is now going to be solved.
zh: 我觉得将继续茁壮成长的工程师基本上会变得更像 PM，因为编码部分现在将被解决。

speaker: Unknown
en: And now it's just, "What should we build?
zh: 现在只是，“我们应该构建什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is this great?
zh: 这很棒吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Is this the direction we want to go?
zh: 这是我们想要去的方向吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: What does success look like?"
zh: 成功是什么样子的？”

speaker: Unknown
en: So if you're like, " Oh shit, my job is changing," just imagine being an engineer right now and how crazy that must feel.
zh: 所以如果你就像，“哦该死，我的工作在改变”，想象一下现在做一名工程师，那种感觉一定有多疯狂。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think the one advantage that engineers have is they think in systems, and they think about obsolescence more effectively.
zh: 我认为工程师的一个优势是他们以系统的方式思考，并且他们更有效地思考过时的问题。

speaker: Unknown
