en: So I think that when you make a change, you have to decide, is this change going to be sustainable in the product that you're offering?
zh: 所以我认为当你做出改变时，你必须决定，这个改变在你提供的产品中将是可持续的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I think an engineer has an edge there.
zh: 我认为工程师在那里有优势。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think the other edge they have is, "I bet you we can build this to be more automated, to be simpler."
zh: 我认为他们拥有的另一个优势是，“我敢打赌我们可以构建这个以变得更自动化，变得更简单。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I think those things-
zh: 我认为这些东西——

speaker: Unknown
en: And the minority goes there.
zh: 少数人会去那里。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 是的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think that every one of us, the product person probably has a little bit of an edge in judgment and communication.
zh: 所以我认为我们每一个人，产品人可能在判断和沟通方面有一点优势。

speaker: Unknown
en: The engineer's going to have a little bit of edge in the system scaling and how does this change affect folks?
zh: 工程师在系统扩展和这种改变如何影响人们方面将有一点优势。

speaker: Unknown
en: The designer's going to have a little bit of an edge with taste.
zh: 设计师在品味方面将有一点优势。

speaker: Unknown
en: And all of these things will still remain important.
zh: 所有这些事情仍然将很重要。

speaker: Unknown
en: And obviously people that have all of these will do better than those that have one or two, but the industry's more than safe, as evidenced by your report that there's more hiring.
zh: 显然拥有所有这些东西的人会比只有一两项的人做得更好，但行业是安全的，正如你的报告所证明的那样，招聘更多了。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think it's going to grow.
zh: 我认为它会增长。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's just different class of individual.
zh: 这完全是一个不同阶层的个体。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is what we're trying to communicate.
zh: 这就是我们要传达的内容。

speaker: Unknown
en: The design piece is really surprising to me.
zh: 设计这一部分对我来说真的很令人惊讶。

speaker: Unknown
en: The fact that this data show that the number of design roles is plateauing, and just how teams are just not valuing design as much as you think.
zh: 这一点数据表明设计角色的数量正在趋于平稳，而且团队根本没有像你认为的那样重视设计。

speaker: Unknown
en: You would think that design becomes much more important as the number of products grows exponentially, that it's a way to stand out, to build something really beautiful, and have a really thought-through experience.
zh: 你可能会认为，随着产品数量呈指数级增长，设计会变得更重要，这是一种脱颖而出的方式，是为了构建真正美丽的东西，并拥有真正经过深思熟虑的体验。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it's interesting that that's not happening right now.
zh: 有趣的是，这现在并没有发生。

speaker: Unknown
en: Also, just...
zh: 另外，就是……

speaker: Unknown
en: I've been thinking about, there's no world where I'm a great designer.
zh: 我一直在思考，我在任何情况下都不可能成为一名伟大的设计师。

speaker: Unknown
en: I can't just use AI and become a great designer.
zh: 我不能直接使用 AI 就成为一名伟大的设计师。

speaker: Unknown
en: Unlike an engineer, maybe a PM, I could become a much better PM with AI.
zh: 不像工程师，也许是 PM，我可以通过 AI 成为一名好得多的 PM。

speaker: Unknown
en: AI is knocking; it will do some cool stuff, but I'm not ever going to feel like I am a great designer now.
zh: AI 正在敲门；它会做一些很酷的事情，但我永远不会觉得我现在是一名伟大的设计师。

speaker: Unknown
en: So it's really interesting that design isn't more successful right now with the rise of AI.
zh: 所以，很有趣的是，随着 AI 的兴起，设计并没有变得更成功。

speaker: Unknown
en: And it might be that we just don't know what to make of design in this era.
zh: 也许可能只是我们不知道在这个时代如何理解设计。

speaker: Unknown
en: That might be a piece of it.
zh: 这可能是其中一部分原因。

speaker: Unknown
en: The other piece might be just like there's product builders and then product information movers, and they're split up.
zh: 另一部分可能就像是，有产品构建者，然后有产品信息搬运者，他们被分裂开了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think that there are pixel generators and then there are tastemakers.
zh: 我认为有像素生成器，然后有品味制造者。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I worry that in design, maybe the industry itself conflates design with more production and not with taste.
zh: 我担心在设计领域，也许行业本身把设计与更多的生产混为一谈，而不是与品味混为一谈。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I think there's a lot of companies that you would talk to, and even when they were hiring head of design, they were thinking more around, "We need more production."
zh: 所以我认为有很多公司你会去和他们交谈，甚至当他们招聘设计主管时，他们考虑更多的是“我们需要更多的生产力”。

speaker: Unknown
en: And great designers are much more tastemakers.
zh: 而伟大的设计师更多的是品味制造者。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I think that is probably... that bias is entering into the hiring plans in this era that we have today.
zh: 我认为这可能是……这种偏见正在进入我们这个时代的招聘计划中。

speaker: Unknown
en: What a wild time we're living through, Nikhyl.
zh: 我们正经历着一个多么疯狂的时期，Nikhyl。

speaker: Unknown
en: Holy moly.
zh: 我的天哪。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, isn't it funny that neither of us are operational?
zh: 是的，很有趣，我们俩都不负责运营吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: There are days when I'm like, "Man, am I so happy I have the time to work on this."
zh: 有些日子我会想，“伙计，我很高兴我有时间来做这个。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And then other days that I think this is some of the most interesting... being operational right now is quite a ride.
zh: 而有些日子我认为这是最有趣的……现在负责运营真是一场过山车。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so there's good and bad.
zh: 所以有好有坏。

speaker: Unknown
en: And COVID wasn't easy.
zh: COVID 并不容易。

speaker: Unknown
en: There was a lot of challenges around working and seeing so much change.
zh: 在工作和看到如此多变化方面有很多挑战。

speaker: Unknown
en: So there's always something, but this one's... everything's being questioned, including how you define joy, which I think is just absolutely fascinating.
zh: 所以总是有事发生，但这一个……一切都在被质疑，包括你如何定义快乐，我认为这绝对令人着迷。

speaker: Unknown
en: Chaos is a ladder, as Littlefinger famously said.
zh: 混乱是一架梯子，正如 Littlefinger 著名地说过的那样。

speaker: Unknown
en: Nikhyl, is there anything that we haven't covered?
zh: Nikhyl，有什么我们还没覆盖到的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Anything else that you think is important for people to hear?
zh: 还有什么你认为对人们来说很重要的事情吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Any other advice, anything you want to double down on before we wrap up and get to our very exciting lightning round?
zh: 还有其他建议吗，在我们结束并进入我们非常令人兴奋的闪电轮之前，还有什么你想加倍强调的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I would just say that there's a lot of room to be optimistic right now, but you have to find it within yourself to recognize that there is a small period of change and exhaustion that's required to find that moment of joy.
zh: 我只想说，现在有很大的空间保持乐观，但你必须在内心找到它，并认识到要找到那个快乐的时刻，需要经历一段短暂的变化和疲惫期。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I just urge everyone to find those reserves to get there, because once you do, it's infectious, but the longer you wait, the harder it is to cross that chasm.
zh: 我只是敦促每个人找到那些储备能量去到达那里，因为一旦你做到了，它是具有感染力的，但你等得越久，跨越那个鸿沟就越困难。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I really want people to feel optimistic and find that moment of joy, but I also recognize that there is a lot of activation energy that it takes to get there.
zh: 所以我真的希望人们感到乐观并找到那个快乐的时刻，但我也认识到，到达那里需要大量的活化能。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so my hope is that you've... and there's a lot of people going through it.
zh: 所以我希望你……并且有很多人正在经历它。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so there's some safety in numbers here, but definitely I urge everyone to try their hand at rethinking their craft.
zh: 所以这里在人数上有一些安全感，但肯定我敦促每个人都尝试重新思考他们的手艺。

speaker: Unknown
en: And just to be clear, you are seeing many people in the community doing well, enjoying this, thriving, this is possible.
zh: 为了明确起见，你确实看到社区中的许多人做得很好，享受其中，蓬勃发展，这是可能的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, it's smiling exhaustion I see in my community.
zh: 是的，我在我的社区看到的是带着微笑的疲惫。

speaker: Unknown
en: And before it was just exhaustion.
zh: 以前只是疲惫。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I take smiling exhaustion over exhaustion, but the pace is relentless.
zh: 所以我选择带着微笑的疲惫而不是纯粹的疲惫，但这种节奏是无情的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't have any...
zh: 我没有任何……

speaker: Unknown
en: There's nothing I can say to sugarcoat that point.
zh: 我无法说出任何话来粉饰这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: What a time to be alive.
zh: 这是一个活着的好时代。

speaker: Unknown
en: Well, Nikhyl, with that, we've reached our very exciting lightning round.
zh: 好的，Nikhyl，说到这里，我们到了非常令人兴奋的闪电轮。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've got five questions for you.
zh: 我有五个问题要问你。

speaker: Unknown
en: Are you ready?
zh: 你准备好了吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Go.
zh: 开始。

speaker: Unknown
en: Go for it.
zh: 问吧。

speaker: Unknown
en: What are two or three books that you find yourself recommending most to other people?
zh: 你发现自己最常向别人推荐的两三本书是什么？

speaker: Unknown
en: You know, Lenny, I'm not a reader.
zh: 你知道，Lenny，我不是一个读书的人。

speaker: Unknown
en: I hate to admit this, but I kind of vibe-code instead of most consumption of content right now.
zh: 我不想承认这一点，但我现在主要是进行 vibe coding 而不是消费大部分内容。

speaker: Unknown
en: All of the reading I do is trying to stay up to date, and that was a casualty.
zh: 我所做的所有阅读都是为了跟上时代，但这却成了牺牲品。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I don't listen to as many podcasts as I want to.
zh: 所以我没有听尽可能多的播客。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't read as much as I want to, because I have so much information coming from the agents that I have deployed and all the stuff that's going on on Reddit and X and all that.
zh: 我也没有读我想读的那么多东西，因为我有如此多的信息来自我部署的智能体，以及 Reddit 和 X 上发生的所有事情等等。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I don't have a great set of book suggestions, not unlike all your other guests that are great at this.
zh: 所以我没有很好的书籍建议，就像你所有其他擅长此道的嘉宾一样。

speaker: Unknown
en: They read prolifically, and I just don't.
zh: 他们读的书很多，而我只是不读。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, I'll tell you, I read...
zh: 我的意思是，我会告诉你，我读了……

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know how much I loved it, and I'll just leave it in here in case people are interested, but I read this book called James, which James is the story of Huckleberry Finn told from Jim's point of view.
zh: 我不知道我有多喜欢它，我会把它留在这里以防人们感兴趣，但我读了一本叫《James》的书，James 是从 Jim 的视角讲述的哈克贝利·费恩的故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: I thought that was absolutely fascinating, because anytime you take a classic and then you look at it through the eyes of someone else, and what you realize is that story is quite powerful, but very haunting.
zh: 我认为这绝对迷人，因为每当你拿出一部经典，然后通过别人的眼睛来看它，你意识到的是那个故事非常强大，但也非常令人难忘。

speaker: Unknown
en: And when you read Huckleberry Finn, you're a kid.
zh: 当你读《哈克贝利·费恩》时，你是个孩子。

speaker: Unknown
en: So the distinction between seeing the innocence, as not only the book was written from Twain's perspective, who was quite forward-thinking, by the way, but you remember you were a kid at the time, and now as an adult, you see the book from another angle.
zh: 所以看到纯真的区别在于，这本书不仅是从 Twain 的视角写的，顺便说一句，他相当有前瞻性，而且你记得那时你是个孩子，现在作为成年人，你从另一个角度看这本书。

speaker: Unknown
en: I thought that was interesting.
zh: 我认为那很有趣。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't know if I'd recommend it to your readers, but I thought I always look for things that I'm... unnatural stories that exist right in plain sight.
zh: 我不知道我会不会把它推荐给你的读者，但我想我总是在寻找那些我……存在于显眼处的不自然的故事。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that was an example of that.
zh: 所以这就是一个例子。

speaker: Unknown
en: I think somebody else has actually recommended that book, so that's a second mention.
zh: 我想别人其实也推荐过那本书，所以这是第二次提及。

speaker: Unknown
en: Next question.
zh: 下一个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you have a favorite recent movie or TV show?
zh: 你有最喜欢的最近的电影或电视剧吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: You've already shared some vibe-coding favorites to be in the background.
zh: 你已经分享了一些适合在背景中进行 vibe coding 的最爱。

speaker: Unknown
en: Anything else?
zh: 还有其他的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah.
zh: 有的。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, I think everyone's talking about Paradise.
zh: 我的意思是，我认为每个人都在谈论《Paradise》。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, if you haven't watched season one and season two, it's on Hulu.
zh: 我的意思是，如果你还没看第一季和第二季，它在 Hulu 上。

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, is season two out?
zh: 哦，第二季出来了吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I didn't realize that.
zh: 我没意识到这一点。

speaker: Unknown
en: Season two is almost wrapping up, and I haven't watched season two yet.
zh: 第二季即将结束，我还没看第二季。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a little apocalyptic in nature, but it's absolute fascinating character drama about how people deal with very, very challenging times and what motivates individuals.
zh: 它有点启示录的性质，但它绝对迷人的人物戏剧，关于人们如何应对非常、非常具有挑战性的时代以及是什么在驱动个人。

speaker: Unknown
en: The other one that I really like is Lioness, which is on Paramount Plus.
zh: 我真正喜欢的另一个是《Lioness》，它在 Paramount Plus 上。

speaker: Unknown
en: Lioness is a story really about a covert CIA group and how they deal with protecting security for America, and the importance and what it means to commit to a bigger cause.
zh: Lioness 真的是一个关于 CIA 秘密小组的故事，以及他们如何处理保护美国的安全，以及致力于更大事业的重要性和意义。

speaker: Unknown
en: I thought that was a really well-written show.
zh: 我认为那是一部写得非常好的剧集。

speaker: Unknown
en: So both of those I recommend.
zh: 所以这两个我都推荐。

speaker: Unknown
en: I do watch a ton of television, and as I mentioned, I vibe-code to certain shows.
zh: 我确实看了大量的电视节目，正如我提到的，我会对某些节目进行 vibe coding。

speaker: Unknown
en: And these two I don't vibe-code to, because I have to pay attention.
zh: 这两个我不进行 vibe coding，因为我必须集中注意力。

speaker: Unknown
en: That's the new bar if something's great.
zh: 如果某样东西很棒，这就是新的标准。

speaker: Unknown
en: Exactly.
zh: 确实如此。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you have a favorite product you recently discovered that you really love?
zh: 你有最近发现的你真的很喜欢的最喜欢的产品吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: Could be an app, could be some gadget, could be something else.
zh: 可能是一个应用程序，可能是某个小玩意，可能是其他东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: My favorite product that I've discovered of late might be kind of obvious to a lot of folks, but I'm not a car guy, so I'll just start by saying that.
zh: 我最近发现的最喜欢的产品对很多人来说可能有点显而易见，但我不是个车迷，所以我就先这么说。

speaker: Unknown
en: Though I've enjoyed riding the newer electric cars, there's a large number of people that love cars, they love driving cars and talking about cars and repairing cars, just like the opposite of me.
zh: 虽然我喜欢开较新的电动汽车，但有很多人喜欢汽车，他们喜欢开车、谈论汽车和修理汽车，就像与我相反。

speaker: Unknown
en: I just want my car to be super reliable.
zh: 我只希望我的车超级可靠。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I've had a Tesla for a really long time, a Model S for just a long time, and I've had it since pretty much they came out with them.
zh: 我有一辆 Tesla 很长时间了，一辆 Model S 很长时间了，自从它们问世以来我就有一辆。

speaker: Unknown
en: My daughter recently passed her driving exam, and because she passed her driving exam, one day my wife said, "Hey, we got to get her a car."
zh: 我女儿最近通过了她的驾驶考试，因为她通过了驾驶考试，有一天我妻子说，“嘿，我们得给她买辆车。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I was like, "We do?"
zh: 我就说，“我们需要吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: I never actually connected the dots that she's going to need to drive a car.
zh: 我从来没有把她需要开车这件事联系起来。

speaker: Unknown
en: And I was like, "Well, when's she going to go?"
zh: 我就说，“好吧，她什么时候要走？”

speaker: Unknown
en: And eventually I'm like, "Okay, maybe we should buy our car, but she's not going to drive that much.
zh: 最终我就像，“好吧，也许我们应该买我们的车，但她不会开那么多。

speaker: Unknown
en: Maybe I should use this as an opportunity to upgrade my car."
zh: 也许我应该利用这个机会升级我的车。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I drove the latest Teslas that are out there, the ones with the self-driving.
zh: 所以我试驾了那里最新的特斯拉，那些带有自动驾驶功能的。

speaker: Unknown
en: And the self-driving works quite well, just to be clear.
zh: 而且自动驾驶运行得相当好，先澄清一下。

speaker: Unknown
en: I mean, I have it now for the last month, and about 95% of the time I self-drive.
zh: 我的意思是，我这车有了上个月了，大约 95% 的时间我都在用自动驾驶。

speaker: Unknown
en: But the most interesting thing is I had no idea that I actually have mild anxiety when I drive that goes away when I self-drive, and I had no idea.
zh: 但最有趣的是，我不知道我开车时其实有轻度焦虑，而自动驾驶时这焦虑就消失了，我完全不知道。

speaker: Unknown
en: And my wife noticed it when we were driving up to the mountains one day, and it was crazy snowing, and I'm like, "We're going to do it."
zh: 我妻子有一天注意到了，当时我们开车去山里，雪下得超级大，我就说，“我们要干这事儿。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And she's like, "You are so anxious when you drive."
zh: 她就说，“你开车的时候太焦虑了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I'm like, "I am?"
zh: 我就说，“我有吗？”

speaker: Unknown
en: Because, Lenny, I've been driving since I was 14 years old, because I grew up in Kansas.
zh: 因为，Lenny，我 14 岁就开始开车了，因为我在堪萨斯长大。

speaker: Unknown
en: And so I passed the test when I was 14.
zh: 所以我 14 岁就通过了考试。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I've been driving literally for nearly 40 years, and I had no idea, as I've gotten older, that this pressing a button and going places is just so awesome.
zh: 所以我实际上已经开了将近 40 年车，我不知道，随着我变老，这种按个按钮去办事的感觉真是太棒了。

speaker: Unknown
en: So anyway, the reason why that experience is interesting, yeah, there's the tech and all that, it's just like I look for ways to reduce my anxiety.
zh: 所以不管怎样，为什么那段经历很有趣，是的，有技术因素等等，这就像我在寻找减少焦虑的方法。

speaker: Unknown
en: Anything that causes anxiety, I try to eliminate at this point in my life, and this is one of them.
zh: 任何引起焦虑的事情，在我人生的这个阶段我都试图消除，而这就是其中之一。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that's a cool product for me.
zh: 所以对我来说这是个很酷的产品。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love that as a rule.
zh: 我喜欢把它作为一个准则。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've also become all in on self-driving.
zh: 我也已经完全投入自动驾驶了。

speaker: Unknown
en: I've had a Tesla for a long time, from the beginnings of their explorations into self-driving, and it was always just like, "I don't know, this is scary."
zh: 我有一辆特斯拉很久了，从他们探索自动驾驶的初期开始，而且一直就像是，“我不知道，这很吓人。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And now it's the opposite.
zh: 而现在情况恰恰相反。

speaker: Unknown
en: My wife prefers I self-drive.
zh: 我妻子更喜欢我用自动驾驶。

speaker: Unknown
en: Even though I drive well, she just feels safer when the self-driving is on.
zh: 即使我开得很好，但当自动驾驶开启时她只是觉得更安全。

speaker: Unknown
en: And there's this... in the actual settings, they give you a stat of what percentage you're driving is self-driving, and they noted it's since 14.2, I think was the release on like that.
zh: 而且这有个……在实际设置里，他们给你一个统计，显示你的驾驶中有多少比例是自动驾驶，他们指出那是从 14.2 开始的，我想那次发布就像那个样子的。

speaker: Unknown
en: So that was the release that everyone started being like, "Wow, it's actually very good now."
zh: 所以那次发布让大家都开始觉得，“哇，现在真的很好了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: And I don't think a lot of people realize that it's actually incredibly good.
zh: 我觉得很多人没意识到它其实非常好。

speaker: Unknown
en: I haven't had a single concerning moment.
zh: 我没有过一次危险时刻。

speaker: Unknown
en: This is one of those examples in product where so many people try the previous versions that when you then tell your friend... a lot of people have Teslas, obviously in California but across the world, when you tell your friends, a lot of people are like, "Oh yeah, I tried it and I turned it off."
zh: 这是产品中的一个例子，很多人尝试了以前的版本，所以当你告诉你的朋友……显然很多人有特斯拉，在加州但在全世界，当你告诉你朋友时，很多人都像，“噢是啊，我试过但我把它关了。”

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think Tesla's got this unusual challenge of getting people to turn it back on.
zh: 所以我认为特斯拉面临着这个不寻常的挑战，就是让人把它重新打开。

speaker: Unknown
en: And obviously the newer hardware is better than the older one, and the newest software.
zh: 显然新硬件比旧的好，还有最新的软件。

speaker: Unknown
en: But once you go through one experience, it can be hard to convince someone to try it again, and that's an adage in product and certainly applies here.
zh: 但一旦你经历过一次体验，很难说服某人再试一次，这是产品中的一句格言，当然也适用于此。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's a downside of releasing early and often sometimes people just have this memory of what it might be.
zh: 这是早期频繁发布的一个缺点，有时人们只是对它可能是什么样有这种记忆。

speaker: Unknown
en: I feel like it's like Uber where you have to just start hearing about it from your friends telling you, "Hey, I'm doing this thing."
zh: 我觉得它就像 Uber，你必须开始从你朋友那里听说，“嘿，我在做这件事。”

speaker: Unknown
en: I'm like, "Wait, really?
zh: 我就像，“等等，真的吗？

speaker: Unknown
en: I guess I should try it."
zh: 我想我应该试一试。”

speaker: Unknown
en: Here's a Tesla suggestion for anyone who's listening is Lenny and I should be able to gift 30 days free to our friends of self-driving, people that have turned it off, because the referral of that plus the endorsement allows people to experience it for free and then they can get their monthly subscription.
zh: 这里有个给任何听众的特斯拉建议，就是 Lenny 和我应该能够给我们的朋友赠送 30 天免费的自动驾驶，给那些把它关掉的人，因为那个推荐加上背书让人们免费体验它，然后他们可以购买他们的月度订阅。

speaker: Unknown
en: Great idea.
zh: 好主意。

speaker: Unknown
en: That viral plus enablement I think is a growth technique that they can have for free from us.
zh: 这种病毒式传播加上赋能我认为是一种他们可以免费从我们这里获得的增长技巧。

speaker: Unknown
en: Such a good idea.
zh: 真是个好主意。

speaker: Unknown
en: By the way, I love Mad Max mode.
zh: 顺便说一下，我喜欢“Mad Max 模式”。

speaker: Unknown
en: I love just the personality of the stuff just... Are you a hurry mode guy or are you a standard mode guy?
zh: 我就喜欢这些东西的个性……你是“赶时间模式”的人还是你是“标准模式”的人？

speaker: Unknown
en: I flip between them, but I do feel like and I'm hoping that the new release of the software keeps us in the lane.
zh: 我在这两者之间切换，但我确实感觉，而且我希望新发布的软件能让我们保持在车道里。

speaker: Unknown
en: I don't love it changing lanes, but it's-
zh: 我不喜欢它变道，但是它是——

speaker: Unknown
en: Oh, like where it's trying to hurry a little bit?
zh: 噢，就像它试图稍微快点那样？

speaker: Unknown
en: Yeah, I wish I could just get it to go at a normal speed, but also don't change lanes because I just don't like moving around.
zh: 是的，我希望我能让它以正常速度行驶，但也别变道，因为我就不喜欢到处移动。

speaker: Unknown
en: Okay.
zh: 好的。

speaker: Unknown
en: Next question.
zh: 下一个问题。

speaker: Unknown
en: Do you have a favorite life motto that you find yourself coming back to in work or in life?
zh: 你有一个最喜欢的人生座右铭吗，你在工作或生活中会经常回归到那个？

speaker: Unknown
en: My high school quote in my yearbook still is my motto, which is, "Genius is 1% inspiration, 99% perspiration," by Albert Einstein.
zh: 我年鉴里的高中引言仍然是我的座右铭，就是，“天才是 1% 的灵感，99% 的汗水，”出自 Albert Einstein。 [[944] 这是某种即使在早期年龄……而且这并不是关于天才是大写的 G。我不是纽约评论员。

speaker: Unknown
en: It's something that even at an early age... and it's not about genius being capital G. I wasn't a New York comment.
zh: 这甚至是很小年纪时就有的东西……而且这跟大写的天才无关。我不是纽约评论。

speaker: Unknown
en: It was more like hard work is really what matters in life.
zh: 它更像是努力工作才是生活中真正重要的东西。

speaker: Unknown
en: What's interesting is, if you read that quote, "Genius is 1% inspiration, 99% perspiration," through the lens of AI, it's absolutely fascinating, because it turns out the AI will do the perspiration quite literally.
zh: 有趣的是，如果你通过 AI 的视角来阅读那句引言，“天才是 1% 的灵感，99% 的汗水，”那绝对令人着迷，因为事实证明 AI 会非常字面地完成那些汗水工作。

speaker: Unknown
en: Unlimited perspiration.
zh: 无限的汗水。

speaker: Unknown
en: So I think what we're leaning towards is we all need to... we're moving to a world where we're all going to be inspired, and it's the inspired individuals in all of us that will essentially continue to proliferate.
zh: 所以我认为我们所倾向的是我们都需要……我们正在迈向一个我们要都被激发灵感的世界，而我们所有人中那些被激发灵感的个体将基本上继续繁衍。
